text:
This actually is the best steak in town!

Tee Off is a place like none other in Santa Barbara. 
Sure their steaks are the amazing (prime rib and bone in rib-eye) but it's the little things that make the short list for date nights, birthday dinners, family in town and just Tuesday. 

First off it's dark. REALLY dark. There are no windows and it was years before I realized how green the place is. I mean, it's golf themed but this place is all green. 

A long bar, which is where you'll find me, is tended mostly by Todd and Matt. Both will shake up a killer martini while a bottle of big, bold red wine sits ready for the main event. The dining room is split with high backed booths up front with a very loungey feel and tables in a back room where there is more light. Also a great room for a surprise birthday party (thank you Sarah S). 

Bring your appetite!
Enjoy a cocktail while you pursue the menu. As mentioned, I suggest red meat but more on that in a moment. The meal starts with a cold veggie tray then it's feeding time!

'Big Bertha' $49 is a massive bone-in ribeye (I leave off the garlic marinade) that comes with Soup (prime rib soup!!!) or Salad, Rice or Potato, Fresh Seasonal Vegetables & Fresh Baked Sourdough Rolls. This is pretty much the only thing I order. Perfect for 2 and you'll have leftovers. Through Covid I've called in a take out a few times and it travels well too. Buyer beware. You'll need a big cutting board to carve this one up. 

Prime Rib $35-54 is the house specially, while they last. Multiple cuts for multiple appetites and again, I dare say, the best in Santa Barbara. Some pork, chicken and a fish or two too but come on. You know why you're here...COW. Moo. 

This is a locals joint where you and team know each other by name and the kitchen crew says "hi" when you walk by. It's on the small side and people tend to linger and enjoy their time so make a reservation on weekends and holidays to ensure you have a seat. If you're lucky, you can slide into the bar and make some new friends. 

Cheers and I'll see you at the Tee Off.

text:
TEN THOUGHTS ABOUT MEATBALLS

1. The space is fantastic. Very bistro-ish.  Open, lots of hard surfaces, so loud, but not obnoxious loud -- festive yet cozy loud. Small bar, but nice cozy little bar. Lots of windows open to Mass Ave.
2. Beer and wine only. 
3. They really need to expand the menu. It really is only meatballs and sides. Granted, two of the sides are salads, and there are brussels sprouts and "seasonal vegetables" (or something like it), but the rest are starchy things like potatoes, pasta and polenta. Maybe some appetizers, some small bites?
4. The salads are unimaginative but surprisingly really tasty (and enough for two to split -- if there are two of you, split the salads and other sides).
5. The service is super friendly and efficient.
6. The meatballs? OK, the turkey was delicate and perfectly seasoned, but the classic is way too dense, and the mushroom sauce is barely even saucy. The much vaunted veggie burger is interesting: crispy on the outside and kind of mushy on the inside; nicely seasoned, but kind of weird, and the suggested spicy cilantro sauce is, well, full of cilantro.
7, The mac and cheese is really good. Creamy. But it needs some . . . mustard? cayenne? Something to punch it up!
8. I have no idea if there are desserts. There should be. None are listed on the menu, none were offered.
9. It's a cool idea, but it's drunk food, right?\
10. The presentation could use a little work.

text:
I was encouraged by the yelp reviews on here, and not deterred by the negative reviews either, so I decided to try it. 
I walk in and there's a guy playing guitar and he's sort of a one-man-band. Pretty impressive guy, its great entertainment. I was here on a Friday night. 
I was standing in the entrance for about 2 minutes before one guy noticed me and got a menu to seat me. 
The place was kinda loud but that's to be expected for a Friday night I guess. I had plenty of time to look over the menu because that same guy who seated me returned and took the order about 10 minutes after I was seated. 
I got water and asked a simple question, whether or not the pollo loco came with beans or not. It didn't so I opted for the standard Carne Asada. I asked for corn tortillas like I always do. The guy disappeared and I noticed the tap water was very awful tasting so I had to flag another waiter down and ask for a lemon (or lime) he was very accommodating and returned immediately with a lime. This made my water way much more tolerable. 
The wait time for the food was very reasonable. about 10-15 minutes. It was brought out by a different person and simply placed on the table. And like that he disappeared. My waiter no where to be seen. 

The food: the carne was not bad. The rice was extremely bland and the beans tasted like they came from a can. If I was able to get the attention of a waiter, I would have asked for Tapatio or Cholula Or Valentino or something to make the rice more palatable. Not one soul returned in 45 minutes or more. (since the person put my dish on the table) So I simply ate it. It usually is customary for a waiter to come check on the table after a third party delivers the food to ask if I need anything. But nope, not here. My original waiter came back after 45+ minutes and asked if I needed more water. I said no, just the check. He gave me the check (the price is VERY reasonable) and didn't return at all. I waited about 10-15 minutes than just got up and saw him in the bar area and asked if I paid at the table or up at the bar. He took the bill and I paid at the bar. 

Very disappointed in the service and the food. The price is right but there may be a reason for that. Granted this guy was probably over worked and understaffed, I only saw two waiters and one waiter (mine) seemed to be pulling double duty as host as well, seating people. So many Mexican options in the area, I will not return here. I loved the artist singing and playing guitar but that was the high point.

This is a fantastic bottle shop! I'm jealous of the fine people of Brandon that they have this gem in their backyard! 

I've visited here 2-3 times now and have been consistently impressed with the quality of bottle and can selection, the variety of stuff in the cooler, and the beers that are on tap for you to sample, drink a pint of, or fill your growler with. The owner is USEFUL, FUNNY, and COOL and his pupper doggo is very nice too. There are tables and seats for you to belly up to while you enjoy your beer. 

We live right up the street (literally) from Jug and Bottle Dept. in Seminole Heights yet we find ourselves making excuses to drive out to Brandon so we can patronize this bottle shop and Tampa Beer Works.

I'm not sure what all the hate is about, maybe because I'm not from St. Louis and didn't know what the Landing was prior to recently, but oh well.

My first experience here was a few years ago for my sisters Bachelorette party - we stayed at Lumiere and partied in the area. It was awesome.

A couple weekends ago we did a beer festival. I will say, the Landing was pretty dead other than those drinking beer, but it was still beautiful. Right on the river, it seemed super clean, and had a GREAT dog park. 

There's a couple spots to eat, a couple spots to go out at night to.

Honestly, I think it's a great spot and can't wait until it starts building up again!

text:
Everything was fine until the microwave. 

Let's start from the beginning. Things were fine when I walked inside. I noticed the very clean salsa bar that is very protected from germs and sneezing. The guy working the counter was young and friendly. It did not smell in there and the menu looked pretty appetizing. I decided on the enchilada combo with extra beans and guacamole. I looked on excitedly to see my food being made. It was off to a great start as it began where every fast food Mexican place begins making the food. 

Then, all of a sudden, in it went into the microwave. It wasn't even hidden. I noticed it when I walked in but thought it could not possibly be used. I was wrong. As orders were going through the window after mine, I saw the same thing. Items going into the microwave. Oh, that horror!

The microwave-prepared food leads me to the taste. It was average. I did not even have to see it go through the microwave to know that it came from one. It wasn't bad, but definitely not great. The guacamole wasn't really guacamole. It was cold. The whole pins were decent. 

Perhaps this place is a go-to for Raspados and Hot Dogs. If you wanted your food to at least taste fresh and non-microwaved, there's a number of fast food Mexican in the vicinity that are better.

Great authentic spot for some great Vietnamese dishes.

I ordered my favorites 
102: Summer Rolls w/ Shrimp and Pork/Chicken (2)
163: Pho w/ Slices of Eye-Round Steak and Beef Balls

I might pass on the beef balls next time but all in all my meal was just what I was looking for. Nam Phuong isn't trying to be anything its not.  It's authentic Vietnamese dishes with no frills. 

This is perfect cold weather food, warm you up and cure you when you're sick food... and cheap cheap good eats.

This ross makes me giggle to walk through. They have a varied selections, and a varied clientele and staff. It's a great hodgepodge of different fun to be had. Depending on the day you might find a great deal on some boots, dresses, or anything really. Or you might see a young couple having a grueling fight, or teenagers stealing things. It's really a plethora of different delights. 

Basically I just shop there, but the people watching can be very epic as well. Going right after work or during lunch can be seriously intense because the lines get out of control, but you'll probably find something to make it worth your stay.

My first time here today!! Not my last! My friends wanted to sit outside today for lunch and we did a bit of debating over where to go. Crown & Bull came up in several of our searches so we decided to try it out. So glad we chose this place because it was amazing for the following reasons:

- FREE TEQUILA SHOT WITH YELP CHECK IN! (The bartender was so awesome in recognizing this perk and the shot was enormous)
- They have something called a manmosa: a pint of mimosa (half of a bottle) for $10 which was well worth every penny
- Amazing outdoor seating area with a huge oak tree as the centerpiece 
- Free parking in town
- Gorgeous, clean restrooms
- Really friendly staff all around and plenty of bar seating if you just want to grab a drink
- Interesting food menu: the Ahi Tuna was outstanding and the wings were really tasty. Everything on the menu was priced around $14-$18

So yeah, the free tequila shot and our fantastic waiters won me over up front, but I feel like there is so much to try here and i need to go back. If this establishment had a happy hour menu, we'd be talking GOLD!!!

Just recently found out about this place that makes legit Detroit style pizza.

Color me shocked and I will for sure be back. Kudos.

text:
Some culinary treasures are simultaneously obvious and obscure.  Chef Vargas' restaurant is one of them.  It's on York Road in Elkins Park in an impossible to miss multi-building apartment complex (I know!) but behind what-appear-to-be service doors at the south end of the complex. The restaurant's only external signage is a small sandwich board outside of the service doors. 

The Mister had the huevos rancheros, which he pronounced "delicious" and requiring a return visit for more, while I had the Caribbean Benedict.  My breakfast consisted of a pile of beautifully seasoned pulled pork sitting atop the most delicious, caramelized maduros I have ever had.  Completing the plate were two flawlessly  poached eggs, a lovely latinized Hollandaise, and crispy-on-the-outside, creamy-on-the-inside home fries.  Next time we visit, I may very well order a bowl of those fantastic maduros.  

Chef Vargas' restaurant is worth seeking out if you are a fan of authentic Latin cuisine.  In addition to the delicious, freshly made food, the restaurant's prices were reasonable, the service was charming and attentive and the coffee was outstanding.

text:
Having lived in UCity for 4 years, my Indian taste palette has been dominated with New Delhi, Ekta, and Sitar. Moving out to the suburbs made me a little nervous to try something new but saw great reviews of 1st Indian Kitchen and decided to take the leap of faith. 

I was not disappointed. Portions were large - easily 2 meals - and the butter chicken had so much chicken we were so surprised! We ordered vegetable samosa, saag paneer and chicken makhani. Saag paneer is the green spinach dish, paneer is the cheese cubes; chicken makhani = butter chicken. Veggie samosa had a lot of peppercorns so it was spicier than I wanted, my husband and I joked that we would be OK if they just gave us fried samosa skin xD But actually. Saag paneer and chicken makhani were VERY good. I'm lactose sensitive - not intolerant - but it didn't give me any stomach issues and it tasted very similar to New Delhi. This is definitely our go-to Indian place from now on. Compared to Saffron (hellaaaa more expensive, the same butter chicken was $17 and I heard it's watery) and Tiffin (their saag paneer is really watered down / not seasoned well) 1st Indian Kitchen is no frills. There's even a tiny parking lot area you can pull up since located along West Chester Pike. Convenient, great prices, efficient customer service, on-time service (we called in our order and it was ready when they said which was 20-30 mins). There is some seating area but very no frills, would honestly say that order take out is probably better (very fast-casual esque) Can't wait to order again!

Never been! Will go again!

Cute little place in midtown. Probably used to be a two or three bedroom house back in the day as many midtown businesses are.

Nice rustic/bbq uplifting decor and super nice staff. Prices are up there but it's to be expected for BBQ. Offerings range from port to need to brisket to bowls!! Tater salad is great, Mac n cheese was amazing. Beverage options include tea, soda and a variety of craft beer.

Just six tables and two pub tables although they do have outdoor seating. There is a sign outside that says they do sell out so get there early and enjoy!!!

Almost (or maybe over?) 2 years now and I can't say enough good things about this place. Every.single.post that I make on SM I always emphazie that its the best ice cream in Tampa Bay. 

Joe, the owner, works so hard to not only keep creating unique flavors (my 2 favs..PB Oreo and Coquito) but to maintain a place that is fun to be in. Even in his staff, everyone seems genuinely happy to be at work and it shows. As a consumer, this is why I always flock to local businesses, especially the ones that really work hard to get and keep your business! 

There is a reason that this place has a 5 star rating!! So, if you like ice cream then your are doing yourself a diservice by not coming here. Ice cream people....ICE CREAM!!!!

text: It's a fun concept, lots of long cafeteria tables, if you have a large group would be great if you get there to snag the space. You stand in line to order food, then they call your name over loud speaker, drinks come out a different area. It's pretty loud inside so every time they call a name, you basically have to stop talking to hear it or else you'll miss it. Several times we had to ask each other in the 15 minutes we waited, "what name was that?" I got the Asian salad which was a large salad and full of goodies, and it tasted good, but it was definitely spicy. On the menu some items did denote spice, this one did not. My friends got the tater tots with garlic and salt. Since it's a beer place I could see how someone could like it, but it was a bit too much for us. The pizza was decent, I would say middle of the road. For a person who eats a lot of pizza, the $20 smaller size would really only feed one person plus a snack for someone else. I don't think it would work for enough food for 2 people. We only got 1 drink because it appeared we would then have to go wait in a line to reorder drinks with everyone else who was just coming in/ ordering their food. The design was fun, and maybe some new kinks. Not sure the set up is for me with the ordering process.

text:
Having been less than wowed at the nearby La Teresita, I was pleasantly surprised by Arco-Iris.  The food was flavorful across the board and the prices were some of the most affordable in Tampa.

Their Cuban bread is quite tasty and any variation of the sandwiches offered here are sure to be good.  I particularly enjoyed their chimichurri as well as their garbanzo bean soup.

Definitely worth a visit.

text:
The Mr was looking for some nice Panama Shirts & there were lots of fun colors in the windows of California Drawstrings that  beckoned us inside....

There were fancier shirts & more casual shirts - all in the same "Panama-style" we were looking for & we ended up getting 2 of the more casual solid colored & 1 fancy striped linen number that were so comfy & cool.

Also saw a bunch of shorts, pants, jackets & hats.....they have it all!

You'll find ladies' wares on the right-side of the shop & men's on the left

The staff was outrageously nice & we had a great time chatting about their upcoming trip to San Francisco - hope yall love it!!

Totally impressed & we'll be back for sure next time we're down South!

text:
Wow, this place is great.  From the food to the decor and the service, we had a great lunch here today.  I had the tagliatelle with sausage and brocoli rabe in a wonderfully oil infused broth.  My wife had the penne alla vodka with grilled salmon.  My parents had the papperdelle bolognese and the frutti mare with papperdelle.
Our meals started with fresh rolls and they were warm and delicious.  My pasta came with the soup of the day which was a butternut squash soup.  It was creamy and luscious.  My wife and mom loved their salads that were dressed with a champagne vinaigrette.  They said the dressing was light and fresh tasting.
Okay, back to the carbohydrate festival.  The pasta of all our dishes were cooked perfectly.  Many like their pasta al dente, and these dishes were just slightly cooked more than that - they way I like it.  All our sauces that were in our dishes were excellent.  My wife's salmon was crusty on the outside and tender on the inside.  My dad's seafood pasta dish was a hit for him - and he's a tough guy to make happy.  I saw his scallops and they were cooked perfectly.
The decor to the place is bright and airy.  The service was excellent.  Our service was bright and fun - this made our meal even better.
We'll be back - for sure!

text:
Hadn't been to Ra in years and recently went with my husband and toddler. Service was excellent and the sushi was even better. So many fun choices too. I'm pregnant and can't have raw fish right now, but was pleasantly surprised with how many options I still had. My son was treated so kindly by the staff and our server, which was an added bonus. Beautiful views if you sit outside too. Would definitely recommend and am craving it again already! I've always felt like the prices were high here, but for fresh fish and cleanliness and the portions, I'll pay!

Bottomline: So many rolls, so little time and not enough room in my belly!

text: If you like Benihana, then you will love this place!  This new location has a great new look.   We're were seated promptly at the hibachi table. categories: Automotive, Oil Change Stations
biz_attributes_clean: None
text: I'm a fan of Jiffy Lube. This one is close to home and I've never had to wait for service (there are three spots), even when going over a lunch hour. They do have a tendency to up-sell you on stuff - definitely ask a few questions as to whether it's really necessary, and price/cost/other options. I have never experienced any pressure to buy the top end product. I've always gotten a really friendly person who is knowledgeable - the last time he taught me how to reset the oil gauge on my dashboard. It's a fairly fast service, probably 20 minutes beginning to end, including their checks of your lights, rpm, etc. I also like that I can collect Air Miles here. I suspect that it costs more to have an oil change here but it's worth it for the convenience!

text: Quite a neat place they have here, plenty of seating and even a game area. My biggest disappointment was their selection of beers on tap. If you like IPAs you can't go wrong here! If you care to drink something else then you may want to check out some other breweries in town. text: Really nice first experience, and looking forward to a return trip. I had heard good things, and we made it our spot for Father's Day lunch. Our service was excellent, and price and ambience were both great. I was generally a fan of the three house beers, which were all good but none super remarkable. We started with the chips and guac, which was good but we needed to ask for more chips twice. We took the recommendation and got the fried mozzarella, which was really top notch. Glad we got that! I enjoyed the beer cheese burger, which was yummy but not the biggest serving. The lasagna was excellent, and the kids menu had lots of excellent choices. Great lunch, looking forward to heading back soon.

text:
I had the unfortunate circumstance of having to cut my trip waaaaaay short while I was in Tucson due to a family emergency so when I arrived to the airport, needless to say, I was flustered and quite the emotional mess. 

The entire staff from checking in with my updated airline booking or the TSA literally handing me an entire box of tissue to take with me to the bartenders in the cheesy airport bar letting me be but keeping my cocktail glass refreshed. I have to admit that I don't recall much about my surroundings BUT I am quite positive that each step of the way at this airport was greeted with kindness and professionalism. As someone who has never been in this kind of circumstance before... I am extremely grateful for that. I hope to be able to come through here again sooner than later!

text:
I visited again during COVID with my son. This has been the first time I'd dined out since COVID began. They set up indoor and outdoor seating with very limited tables/seats available. They use texting to your phone instead of pagers to inform your waitlist status. We arrived at 7 pm and were seated outside at 7:40. We were allowed to go inside to get a beer at the "no seats available" bar. We took the beers outside while we waited. They do practice good sanitation and COVID mask wearing and social distancing only allowing one way traffic through the restaurant area. 
We ordered the "steamed" potstickers which arrived quickly, but unfortunately not very well microwaved. Maybe the fried versions would have been better. The inside was cool of several meaning they didn't quite reheat well enough. During these times, I wasn't comfortable sending it back hoping it was still safe to eat. My son ordered the Black & Bleu Burger while I ordered a half rack of ribs. The ribs were very good which a very nice serving size. 
Our service was very good, but somehow I ended up paying for someone else's drinks at the end of the night (at least 1 drink). I didn't catch the mistake until after I'd left. 
This is definitely a 2-3 star rating type place. Great beer and typical comfort type food. The atmosphere is what makes this chain restaurant.

text: I stopped in to Bellacino's on a whim the other day. Though it is in the same center as Amber Indian, we have never ever been here. On the day of my visit, it was very crowded with workers from the nearby office complexes. Everything looked and smelled great. I chose a chef's salad for carry-out. While I was waiting, I had the chance to case the place a bit. I noticed a few menu items that were unusual, such as the taco grinder (a.k.a. sub or hoagie), the BBQ pork grinder and a reuben grinder....all very interesting, especially the taco grinder. Not sure I would every choose that, but still interesting. My salad was quite good, full of fresh lettuce, turkey, ham, shredded cheese and all the other items that make for a good lunch-sized chef salad. I did think the pizzas going out to the tables looked really tasty, too. Not so tasty, though: the delivery guy who returned while I was waiting; I watched him take about six drags on his cigarette before dismounting his ride and coming back inside. Surefire appetite suppressant right there. Also, I give a one-star demotion for the smudged windows, sloppily dressed employees and stained carpet. My mom always had a hard and fast rule of not allowing our family to set foot in a restaurant with dirty windows when I was a kid....dear God, I AM my mother!! Seriously, Bellacino's, clean up your act a little and I might be a fan :-)

text:
Had dinner at Double Knot for our anniversary - decided to do the tasting menu (chef's choice) to get the full experience. Our night was... very mixed. 

Atmosphere: 3. Seemed fine? Didn't see much of the restaurant, as we were seated at the sushi bar (didn't love that, as it was a special occasion, but if you like watching chefs work their magic it could be fun). Dark. Sound level was perfect (rare in a Philly restaurant). 

Service: 4. Our servers were all very attentive and informed. Plates came out WAY too fast, though - WHY?!? We asked them to slow down after we were delivered ~5 plates in the first 20 minutes. And had to ask AGAIN as they brought out another plate. 

Food: 3. There were some truly stand-out dishes, and some that we would never order again. Small plates (especially sashimi, robatayaki) were EXCELLENT - we are already talking about coming back for cocktails and snacks. Unfortunately, the meal went downhill from there. We didn't love the mushroom dumplings. The roasted brussel sprouts were like little oil-bombs, just drenched in oil. Our two entrees (scallops and pork tenderloin) were cooked fine but completely overwhelmed with soy sauce, oil, and unnecessary garnish. Dessert (mochi) was forgettable. If we could have stopped the meal at the robatayaki (venison and swordfish, equally wonderful) we would have left happy. 

Drinks: 4. I was unimpressed with the two glasses of wine I tried, but my partner ordered the double knot cocktail - and it was every bit as impressive as you would think. A little smoke, and a TON of flavor, perfectly balanced. His new favorite cocktail in Philly. 

Cost: 2. We are glad we did the tasting menu to get a feel for the range of options; but will not do it again, both based on the food and the cost. Stick to cocktails and small plates.

We would never have gotten this far in 6 weeks without Matt's help. If you have a new puppy and are feeling lost & hopeless like we were, you need Tuff Pup in your life!

text: Seriously THE best pumpkin supreme pancakes I've ever had! I like that they are directly across the street from me so I can head over for the pancakes in the fall! You can get them either full size (which i dont recommend unless you have a HUGE appetite) or the smaller version (which is what i get). In addition to these always tender pancakes having the perfect balance of pumpkin spice and flavor is the deliciously rich caramel pecan sauce that comes with it (i always ask for extra because i like my pancakes drowning in sauce or syrup). Sometimes it cones with whipped cream, sometimes it doesn't, either way is fine, I guess it depends on who's cooking. I enjoy a generous portion of always crispy bacon to balance out the heady sweetness of the pancakes. Definitely a place i look forward to coming to during the fall/winter season!

text: Old Town Tavern is a downtown Carmel staple that could be considered a rite of passage if you grew up in the area. It's a no-frills dive bar with its fair share of regulars. The drinks are cheap and the inside is small but that's what gives it character. One dart board, one pool table. Very limited seating. Friendly people. The biggest downside for me is that it is a smoking bar, although I'm sure those who smoke enjoy the handful of bars that still allow it. I usually find myself here around the holidays when old friends are back in town.

text:
I was looking forward to coming here, and honestly I had to hustle and cut another meet up short, and pay for transportation to make it here on time for my appointment (instead of walking as I had planned on a nice day), only to have them be unprofessional and make me wait over 30 minutes because their therapist was running late. I'm thankful that I was able to push back other things to still get it done today, but honestly it is unfortunate that I had to wait so long for a simple 20 min brow tint. 

However, they seem to use really good products, and once they finally saw me, they were attentive and personable. I had a coupon so I was excited to try out the brow tint, but to be honest, I'm not sure if it's worth the price if I had to pay full price for it. My eyebrows are already fairly dark, so the tint was more to highlight the baby hairs, but it didn't do too much for me. Also, it stained my skin around it much more than I anticipated based on what she told me, which did not look too flattering as it was fairly uneven. 

Just personally didn't have much of a wowed experience here but can't complain much either!

text:
We had a reservation for late afternoon on a Sunday but were seated at a tiny table near the bathroom and kitchen. Asked if we could move but were discouraged because they said kids in the area with open booths were very noisy and disturbing other guests. Wish we had ignored them as a short while later the piano bar started up. We never heard a peep from the kids the entire time and when their party left before us they all filed out quietly without any fuss. Between the piano and people/servers walking very close to our table all night going to and from the restrooms and kitchen, it was extremely noisy and disruptive. The only other table offered was a bit bigger but right in front of the piano and only a few feet from the table we were at. They did not care and did not offer to try and find us another table, even though it took quite a while for our food to come out. Surely other tables would have opened up.

The stuffed mushrooms with crab are not actual stuffed mushrooms but a mushroom casserole - the description does not make that clear. When we asked about it they said we should have Googled it before coming. It was mostly spinach and cheese, only some unappetizing grey mushrooms and barely any crab - see pix (was $17 but now $19) . We were told that the appetizer got lost for a while before it arrived, so it was not hot. Other items were okay but sadly for us nothing was really great. I probably should have stuck with the pasta entrees. The portions of game meat on the platter were small and some pieces were dry. It was not very warm either. Total bill was $120. 

I gave 2 stars since my companion's meal was not bad and the server was nice, although clearly rushed and uncomfortable about talking with management about our situation. We were just given negative answers and nothing was done. I wrote a review on Google to which the owner responded to say that I was lying and how honest they are. Not sure that's the best approach but it demonstrates that they apparently can't deal with any negative feedback, whether at the restaurant or in reviews. I've written hundreds of positive reviews and have no reason to write a negative review for them without cause. They claimed I did not mention anything while at the restaurant, which was not true, and sadly they took no responsibility at all.

I'll save you the suspense --the street vendors around the city serve better halal chicken with much more complex and varied flavors . Amd the yogurt and hot sauce here taste like ...well , nothing . The hot is just hot --no vinegar balance --and the yogurt tastes more like mayonnaise or ranch dressing . The biryani chicken is meh , the rice was fluffy , but I found myself wanting a better balance of flavors . I asked if they had any other sauces --or even just kitchen vinegar or lemon or lime .

They didn't . Kind of weird but whatever .

I agree with the other reviews that mentioned that it has a weird mish mash of flavors and it can't decide what it wants to be , but definitely does not taste like any authentic Indian food I've had before .
Not bad , just not my jam .

text:
I really like Mari Chuy's, I've been to their midtown location and loved it,  since I was planning to see a movie at the next door theater, we opted to try the Sparks location. 

Being COVID times, we opted to sit on the patio which had tables nicely spaced out. The venue across the way was playing live music, so it was a really nice atmosphere. 

I ordered a Strawberry Margarita and Chiles Rellenos Con Queso both excellent. Since I love uber spicy food, the waiter brought me some Ghost Pepper sauce in a bottle (so good). Rellenos were made with fresh ingredients, the eggs where light and fluffy, and the dish was cooked perfectly (some restaurants really mess this dish up - Mari Chuy's does it perfectly).  The Margarita was strong, refreshing, and had a light strawberry flavor.

Our waiter was extremely friendly, helpful, and professional. 

Only 'bad' thing about Mari Chuy's is their chips and salsa is ADDICTIVE! So addictive! I had enjoyed way toooo many chips with salsa. How dare they tempt me with such delicious apps. ;) 

Lastly, I like how Mari Chuy's makes an effort to be inclusive to vegetarians and vegans, their vegetarian section of the menu has several non-typical additions (not just bean and rice burritos) and several are vegan.  100% recommend them.

After Ming Dynasty closed, it was time to search for another Chinese restaurant in the area.  I was pleasantly surprised by China King.  I honestly don't get out to that part of Goleta as much, but I'm glad I gave it a shot.

The standout dish here was the orange peel beef.  I especially love when restaurants include the burnt orange peel with the beef itself, and China King's had plenty of that (as well as roasted spicy peppers).  This is frankly better than the one that was at Ming Dynasty in my opinion.  The chow mein was less impressive, but I'll probably just end up trying something else next time as there are plenty of options from which to choose.

Overall, nice to know that there's another game in town.

text: Solid brunch place in NoLibs, it's a little bit off the beaten path which I like because it keeps it a little hidden gem. There are about 20 seats maybe inside and about that many outside. It was pretty hot the day we went so we decided to sit inside. The servers were knowledgeable and nice and the service was attentive. For food we went with the cinnamon pull aparts, the brunch burger, and the egg sandwich. Everything was really, the burger blend they use is really good, the burger had a good beefy flavor and the bacon is just awesome, I would just get a side of that. The potato roll it was on held up well and the breakfast sandwich was also very good. Definitely a good place to hit for brunch we will have to wander in here one night have a dinner.

text:
The guys behind Imperial Lounge, Lincoln Lounge and Old Granite Street eatery have opened a wine and whiskey bar called Royce. I'm impressed with their selections of both, although I wonder if Reno is the right market for a place that sells hipsterish wine.
Not all the wines are natural, but they skew towards lower alcohol, organically produced wines. The prices are pretty good, too. The conventional wisdom is that you sell a glass of wine for close to what the bottle sells for at retail. They have the Luneau Papin Muscadet for $8 a glass. It retails for $13 to $15 a bottle. Maybe by selling a tasty white at such a low price they are trying to kick start the market for people who have never tried it. (Don't confuse the dry Muscadet from the Loire Valley in France with the grape muscat, often sweet). Teutonic Riesling from Oregon is $12 a glass and is another good value.
The tails of natural wine lovers will start wagging when they can get PUR syrah for $8 a glass. Au Bon Climat pinot noir is only $8 a glass too. My only complaint here is that even Clendenen's inexpensive wines need some bottle age to show well and even though it's a 2011 it might be too young.
By-the-bottle choices include La Pierre, Broc Cellars, and Clos Rougeard  for the reds and two excellent whites, Texier, Occhipinti. Those last two really make those tails wag.
But the real coup is Allemand. I don't think it's on many wine bar menus in San Francisco or New York or even restaurant menus. I wonder if the boys scored a case or two and have it on the menu as a trophy. At $160 a bottle, I wonder if they sell much. Part of the problem may be the people in Reno who can afford that are geared more to the ripe, oaky Napa cabs. Allemand can be floral, elegant, sometimes smoky. It is nothing like the typical trophy Napa cab. But it's cool that we have a place in Reno selling Allemand.
I just got into whisky this year. They have several good single malt and Japanese whiskies. I could get seriously hammered trying everything they have there that is good.
You can order appetizers from Old Granite Street Eatery next door. Those are usually pretty good.
I went there for the Beaujolais Nouveau tasting. For the price of a glass of wine they had a nice table of goodies, charcuterie, cheese, crackers and fruit. It was a nice spread. 
I like the idea of Royce. It joins the West Street Wine Bar as the two best places in Reno to get a glass of wine. I hope the market is here to support it.

I needed 2 pair of pants hemmed with a side cut in less than 4 hours.  I called and actually thought I would get a voice-mail but the owner answered.   I explained the situation and time frame and she told me she could have my pants done before 5 pm.  I was at I-70 and Keystone exit.  I quickly turnaround and 15 minutes later after parking on a metered space behind Whole Foods, I was trying on the pants for hemming.   

I quickly pulled on a pair of pants for sizing and the owner literally only needed one needle to get the hemming down pat.  As promised, I arrived at 5pm and she had 2 pair of pants ready with a side cut.  I was able to show up to my commitment on time and properly dressed

If you need something hemmed quickly, do not hesitate to contact the owner for the best service!

This library (and perhaps the system as a whole) has really ramped up its efforts to stay connected with the community throughout the pandemic, which is definitely laudable. They've completely converted to curbside pickup and had some opening hours during the summer (with social distance and masks required), and they've done a lot virtually as well.

text:
We went to the buffet during lunch time and this is near the Western Village Casino.  You can order the lunch menu which runs from 11am to 4pm at $19.99 or the dinner menu which runs for $29.99

For dinner, you get beef tongue, black angus New York Steak, Boneless Short Rib, Galbi.  Now was it worth it for the extra price, you bet however, I can not indulge in extra beef due to gout.

Here is what we ordered and my quick opinion
1.  Fried Wonton - definitely get this, we got 2 orders of thoese
2. Green Salad - I love the sesame dressing
3.  Fried Oyster - get this one
4. Woo Sul - the beef tongue was tender. My wife loved this one
5. LA Bonless short rib - must have
6. Galbi - was very yummy, nice and yuicy and loaded with fat
7.  Porky Belly - must have as well
8.  Beef Intestine - I did not care too much for this one as it was rubbery, but my wife loved this

Service was great they got our food in and out for the most part.  The only thing we had to wait for was the ice cream.   The floors were not sticky or slippery as some Korean BBQ places some are and you are afraid to slip.  Maybe it is because we were the first ones there.

One thing to know is you still need to make reservations, however, we did come earlier than expected and they were able to seat us.  Overall, it was a great experience.

text:
BUYER BEWARE!

Ordered a chicken cheesesteak from here tonight with fried onions and got the absolute worst chicken cheesesteak I've ever had. Their chicken is not chicken steak but HUGE chunks of unflavored unmarinated grilled chicken slopped on a chewy old roll with very little cheese on the roll and not the actual meat. I don't waste food, I REALLY don't, but this was immediately spit out and thrown in the garbage. I ended up ordering another sandwich from a place down the road (which means I've spent over $25 for one meal). Please, for the love of God, don't order a chicken cheesesteak from this place. text:
I really want to give more stars, because I really like the concept...but unfortunately it falls short! The good news is it is new...so I hope improvements can be made. Like I said I love the concept of a community market like this with multiple food options, cooking classes and live music.  I also like the little shop as you enter...displaying different treasures by local artists. There is a bar area in the middle with local beers on tap. So where do they fall short? I can't get comfortable here, feels like I'm in some bizarre coffee shop in the middle of a grocery store or something. The lights are super bright and there are little kids running and screaming. Overall not a chill relaxing place to grab a drink. It definitely wouldn't be "a destination" place for me, but I would still probably come in if I was early to a movie or something. 

Again, concept is good....I hope they fix the vibe and ambiance...because it is a good start to what could be a solid product.

text:
Valentine's Day menu - 3 courses for $39.50/each.  We had the Caesar salad, a 16 oz (!) ribeye, the triple cut lamb chops, and the chocolate-covered strawberries.  

The place was PACKED.  Wall-to-wall people.  We got our (tiny) table in the center of the main dining room.  Service was hurried, at best.  We were handed a menu and when our server returned with the wine menu, we were asked "so, are you ready to order?"  We had the menus for one minute, at most.  Literally one minute.  When I asked for more time, she came back within 45 seconds to see if we made up our minds.  Yikes.

I ordered a bottle of the 2005 Twomey Merlot.  At $60, it was a steal.  Total Wine has it at 49.95!  It pays to be a total wine geek at times.  I mean, it was between the Twomey and the Shafer.  Both retail at $50, but the Shafer carried a $90 tag - which, in terms of steakhouse markups, is still a steal!

So, rushed ordering aside, the salads came and were passable.  Nothing great but certainly not bad in any way.  The lettuce was slightly wilted, showing the salads were made in advance.  

The entrees came within maybe 30 seconds of the salads being moved off of the table.  It felt like we were in a diner.

The steak was tender, well-marbled, and cooked perfectly.  The lamb was slightly overdone but delicious nonetheless.  Juicy, well charred, and mostly medium rare.  The mashed potatoes and mushroom caps were done perfectly.  As good as anything you'd find at the other major chains like Morton's or Ruth's Chris.  In fact, the food was on par with the best steak chains just less pricey with this special.

The desserts...well, this is why the night bordered on 3.5 stars.  We got the chocolate covered strawberries.  What we received were two (tiny) strawberries half dipped in chocolate and served in some watery, milk-based sauce.  I mean, it looked like an amuse bouche.  Seriously, no chef should let that leave the kitchen  

My girlfriend got a rose from a server to end the night.  A nice touch.  

Total time at dinner: 59 minutes.  About right at a diner...not a top steakhouse on a "special" night.

categories: Automotive, Car Wash
biz_attributes_clean: BusinessAcceptsCreditCards: False
text:
I've been here several times and my conclusion is that for $3 bucks and a $1 tip to the people toweling off your car it's a fine deal for a wash.  As Megan M. says, definitely not the best wash ever, but for budget prices and a convenient South Philly location, you get an adequate wash.

As "Mr. X" says, it's true that the service isn't stellar.  The guys running it can be somewhat rude, waving and yelling at you to pull up, pay up, etc.  But, unlike "Mr. X" says, I've always just handed them $3 dollars and asked for the basic wash.  No one has ever asked me to pay more or skimmed any money as far as I know.

Also "Mr. X," there IS a drying section to the automated wash, but toweling off is still standard at every car wash I've visited.  Why are you even tipping $2 if you think you're getting bad service?  I have gotten great service by the people that towel off at other places where they shine your hub caps, windex inner and exterior windows, etc.  You don't get that service here and they only towel off for a minute or two, so don't pay for it.  Just give them $1 and go away with your $4 buck total wash without complaining about the extra fees that don't exist.

They also have power vacuums out front that cost $1 (in quarters) for 5 minutes which is just about enough time to do your entire car.

Unless there's a line you're in and out in just a few minutes.  And four dollars is cheaper than a lot of fancy car washes that can be twice to three times the price, therefore I'll probably frequent this place for a quick, cheap wash.

Southern food... Good for the soul, but not for the gut. You've been warned before stepping on that scale the day after eating here. 

This place is always packed now that they have a TV show about it. I went here a long time ago too. I can't believe I never reviewed it. 

They have a changing menu per the day. Some days chicken, some days ox tail. So just because you don't like the food one day, you might want to come back. Now as far as their staples. Their Mac N Cheese!   I could probably come and just eat this. It's absolutely yumminess melt in your mouth goodness. I also had the smothered pork steak. This thing was so tender you wouldn't even need the fork to cut it. Yum! 

If you want to try Mississippi style Southern food, then this is your place!

text: I'm not sure I can add anymore to what's already been said/written about Fast Eddie's- it's an Alton institution and has seen more patrons over the years than most other establishments. I've been craving sushi for some time. You know that feeling of finally getting what you want and it not measuring up? Well you won't get that feeling from Sake Hana!! Wow what a delightful meal! My boyfriend got beef fried rice & a pork spring roll. I got the sushi and calamari. I'm the sushi fan; he is not. I got a variety of rolls for him to try, but sadly, he said it wasn't for him. That only meant one thing: more for me. I did try some of his fried rice... holy Moses I think that's the best fried rice I've ever had. It's got this grilled/smoky flavor to it and it's just so good!!

The sushi was so so so good; better than I expected. We have enough left over to enjoy again. The Black Horse roll was superb. I thoroughly enjoyed the zap of pepper and the sharp heat of the jalapeño, too. 

We'll have to come back once the pandemic is taken care of!!

Thank you for your great food, Sake Hana!!

text: Artisan Exchange hosts a group of local vendors, many of which have kitchens onsite, at their pop-up open air market every Saturday from 10 AM to 1 PM. Some of my favorites are JamOnIt who has excellent homemade breads, nuts, matzo ball soup, and other delicacies, and What's Up Cream Puff, who has amaretti cookies that are better than any Italian bakery anywhere. Figment Creamery is a new vendor with excellent sorbets and the tortilla chips from Cocina Dotada are yummy. My wife loves the spelt grain snacks when they have them and the coffee they have every week. There's also a popcorn vendor, one that does crab cakes and seafood, an Irish one that does scones, soda breads, and the like, a biscotti and dessert vendor, and a spirits one. They also have vendors that serve savory and sweet pies, an African foods vendor, baked goods, a florist, Italian foods, Turk's Head hot sauces (yum), and an Australian one serving pies that are ready to eat and other desserts. Many vendors have foods that are frozen that you can prepare at home and almost every week they have a featured vendor that serves a hot lunch. If Phyllodelphia is the featured vendor, their lollipop lamb chops are to die for, and Buenos Aires Empanadas does a heavenly skirt steak (entraña) sandwich with chimmichurri. Dragonfly Catering also has great chicken and waffles. And sometimes if you're really lucky you can snag some ready to eat hummus with delicious toppings like lamb or beets from Dizengoff in Philly or Federal Donuts by the half dozen. To make sure you know who will be there on a particular week, sign up for Atrisan's weekly emails. Every Thursday afternoon a list of the vendors and who will be serving lunch comes out. So don't delay..sign up now!

text: Talk about a steakhouse. I saw the biggest portion of prime rib be served to others than ever before. It was unreal. The place is super homey and what you'd think of for an old school steakhouse (the three life size cows out front add a nice touch). Staff was friendly, Jess much more than the other servers. House wine reasonably priced at $20 a bottle and very good. My husband and I split the bone in rib eye, adding on the nibblers plate so we each got our own salad, soup, bread and side. The steak was phenomenal and my husband loved his loaded potato. Bread and salads were great as well, however soup was very salty. Overall we had a great experience. I'd highly recommend splitting entrees unless you really enjoy leftovers!!

text:
This is one place I always pop into while I'm around Whyte Ave.  I'm not too sure why, they don't always have great things, but I guess it's because of the history of the store.  This place has been around for a pretty long time.  Right now they have a lot of Canadian clothing (items with the maple leaf on them) because of the Olympics.  If you want to represent Canada then maybe you could find something you like, a sweater, a hat, or a pair of sweatpants.  They have some okay looking tops but nothing that really jumped out at me and made me feel like it was a must have.  They have some sexy underwear right now since Valentine's Day is coming up but I seem to be of a bigger bust then the bars that they carry.  

I do say the one thing I really like about Army and Navy is their shoe section.  They have some nice shoes and boots on for a pretty decent price.  Now that spring is coming our way soon they have some nice and sexy looking high-heals.  They also have come cute looking flats in some interesting colors (like orange).  

They also have a whole downstairs area if you need camping gear or something for the home (like blankets).  They also have a men's wear section but I really didn't look too much into it so I really don't know what they have.

I guess I would say that this place is a good place to pop into, walk around and see if they have anything that catches your eye.

text:
Best playlist in the city. All 90s R&B is the perfect way to enjoy some amazing Asian fusion! 
What can be said about Rook that hasn't already been said? The food is always top notch, try the Jalapeño Spam and the Fried Avocado buns as an appetizer along with the Bulgogi Dumplings. Also the Beet Fries are delicious, I loved the sauce that came with them. All are so mouthwatering delicious that you will wish there were more to share. For dinner, try the Ramen, as it is probably the biggest bang for your buck and is very flavorful with enough to take home for seconds. 
Their cocktails are also great and they have a good sized wine list as well. 
Street Parking is fairly generous around this part of town, and it's free so make sure you visit soon.

text: I really wanted to like this place despite the mixed reviews. But my experience here did not start out well. I was with my mom who is visiting from out of town my mother-in-law also visiting and my husband. The hostess guided us to a four top table where the seat I was about to sit in was filled with water and food from the previous guests. Thankfully I noticed it before I sat down and had the hostess move us to a booth instead. We ordered our meals and the food came promptly.  I ordered a chicken dish with a thai type sauce and rice broccoli and a side of black beans. Sadly only the black beans were hot. I was so hungry and didn't feel like dealing with it so I ate it as is anyway. My mom asked for a cup of ice and it took our waitress three times before she finally brought it to the table. I'd be willing to give this place another shot but they need to work on delivering all the food to the table hot and paying attention to detail

text:
Before I begin my review, let me insert my new "automatic" disclaimer for all Yelp reviews that I write in the post-COVID world: I've noticed that a lot of my reviews have become slightly less enthusiastic compared to how they were two years ago. However, I'm not lowering my standards until you lower my price. As long as restaurants continue charging me full or even inflated prices, then the least I can do is to expect reasonable service and food quality.

None of those disclaimers is needed at the Rusty Bucket, however. How this little gem escaped me all these years is beyond me. On the surface, this place appears to be just another bar/restaurant located in a strip mall off of a busy street. However, the food quality and the service were outstanding. Every single item we ordered was phenomenal. My pizza was especially fresh and flavorful, and this was an experience that only got better with each passing minute. I didn't want it to end, even though I was stuffed by the time the desserts came out. To say that I'll be coming back here would be an understatement.

text:
This is another cool brewery that came from a humble homebrewer. Just last year, the owner was in the Nola On Tap homebrewer competition and won first place! Not a easy task!  Now he has a real brewery. Dreams do come true!

What really impressed me is they opened up with 16 beers ready to go! I mean they came out the corner swinging!  I heard they took the time to dial in the brews and even had to dump out entire batches because it wasn't right.  They are serious about the quality of the beer.  

They had pretty much all varieties of beer represented.  Even a cider and sours.   I think at this point they were more the straight versions. Meaning a Belgian Wit is has the appropriate ingredients like coriander.  Nothing crazy. Maybe crazy later.  They serve flights , which is really the way to go if you wanna try them all in one visit.  

The staff is cool and very approachable.  Great with kids too.  They sometimes do table service and bring the credit card machine with them.  Very convenient.

I really really dig this venue!   I heard that they plan on fully utilizing the space in the future for bands and maybe burlesque??!?!   If I was to ever open up a brewery, I hope my location is alot like this place!! So damn cool!  Definitely visit Zony Mash.  Which btw is a Meters reference. Gotta love the Meters!!

text:
After an hour drive with a two-month-old baby, we arrived promptly for our appointment. Despite it being  loud and crowded with people, my wife and I were determined to make the best of it.

Just as she was getting ready to change the baby into her outfit, I overheard another customer complaining about how long he had been waiting. Turns out they were about an hour behind schedule.

Judging by the rest of the bad reviews this place has, it clearly isn't a new issue. I am not sure what the bottleneck is. I assume it is for the photo-selection process afterwards, but who knows... We cancelled our appointment. Whoever had an appointment after us probably thought it was a Christmas miracle!

In the end, we ended up going with JCPenney Photo Studios for our photos. Unlike Portrait Innovations, they seem to know how effectively manage their business.

A lot of people seem to be going to Portrait Innovations because of discounts, coupons, etc. I suppose if you have a child that doesn't mind sitting still for hours before having photos taken and you don't value your own time, it might be a good deal. For everyone else, save yourself the frustration and go somewhere else.

text:
We have dined here at least 4 times and i ordered large orders for parties at work. First i want to say, the party tray's that i ordered were a huge hit at my work !!! i highly recommend you put in an order like that for your next function!!! I ordered 50 samosa on both occasions, the second time around i asked to kick up the spice factor, and wow they hit them perfectly on the spot !!!spicy enough to make you sweat a little while not overpowering any of the other flavors. I also ordered a large try (fed 50) of chicken 65 which was Phenomenal !!! Again i asked to go heavier on the spices as many of my coworkers are from India, and they like some heat !!! Again the spice level was perfectly balanced so you could taste al the flavors without being overpowered by any one flavor.

We stopped in midweek for some Manchurian chicken one night and curry goat. Both were incredible and extremely fresh, again with perfect spiciness. The Kheer is awesome too, they have two varieties you can ask for, one is Indian and the other is Pakistani, both are delicious and should be tried. 

Lastly, i'd like to write a bit about the lunch Buffet. Honestly, Masala Zone has one of the best lunch buffet's around, and it cannot be beat. They have awesome Chai unsweetened which i prefer, and incredible mango lassi. They have many chutney's to choose from and a platter of freshly cooked tandoori chicken and Seehk Kabob.....tastes much like Kofta kabob which may be one of my favorite things to eat. They also have chilli chicken, goat curry (personal fav) tikka masala, and many others to try. Everything at the buffet is super tasty and well maintained. never empty foods or food sitting for long as they are constantly making sure everything is full and fresh. 

The restaurant is spotless as is the entire kitchen, and the staff are outstanding and extremely attentive to anything you may need. Do yourself a favor and stop in for a bite to eat !!! I'm sure you will love it as much as i do.

text:
As you can see, I previously gave Amada a 5 star review, and I kept coming back time and time again with delight. Unfortunately, this weekend I saw a different side of the restaurant. Nearly everything has gone up in price, and the portion sizes went way down. We ordered 10 dishes for the table, and more than half of them were tiny and skimping on the ingredients. For example, the clams and chorizo dish is $12, and it came out in a tiny dish with some broth, 3 or 4 miniscule clams, about 5 or 6 completely empty clam shells, and 2 small slivers of chorizo. We didn't know what to do with this dish or how to eat it as a group of four people. 

The spicy potatoes used to be a larger bowl that could be shared. Now it is just two or three small slices of potato spread out on a plate. The octopus ($13) was 5 or 6 tiny slivers of octopus with 2 or 3 slivers of potatoes. The meal came out to be nearly $300 total for 4 people with drinks, so we were hoping for more, especially since we are regulars who have been coming to the restaurant for years.

They used to have a $55 tasting menu that would leave you completely stuffed. That menu is now $65. I understand that you have to raise prices in a world where costs are climbing. However, the portion sizes have become so extremely small that I feel completely ripped off after this meal.

text:
Wow. Happy to have a neighbourhood haunt THIS tasty!

I'd heard through the grapevine they were able to serve gluten friendly meals and they were MORE than able!  EVERY vermicelli dish was able to be served gluten-friendly! So spoiled for choice, I ordered the #86 with chicken (she said you get more than the beef...good choice!). I ordered the Combination #5 for my other half because I know he likes to try ALLLLL the things.  Also ordered salad rolls - more on those later!  The only item not gluten friendly was the crispy spring rolls.

Arrived for pickup and waited outside - the Gateway doors are locked in the evenings, but they will let you in - use the door right next to the parkade ramp to get in - just off the parking lot and there is a bright Open sign to direct you to the right corner of the building.

Prices were really good for take-out.  I always tip 20% for take out during the pandemic... and it was still an affordable meal for 2.

The meal was ready to go when I arrived - I was in the parking lot when I ordered, so I waited the 15 minutes before going inside to pick up.  I'm not rushing a restaurant by staring at them, nor am I going inside with unmasked patrons if I don't have to.

Got my order and headed home.  didn't open the bag or double-check. got home and unloaded the FEAST!

Holy wow portion size! There was enough for two or three meals EACH!  The salad rolls - these were ROUS - rolls of usual size! HUGE long salad rolls filled with pork and shrimp.

Both meals were really tasty.  Headsup for spicy alert - I must have missed it on the online order - #86 has a few dried chili peppers... but not so much I wouldn't eat it!  I tasted Combination #5 - also delicious - very tender beef!

We are looking forward to our next order.  A large diverse menu, and two meals from one order will keep you coming back to try different things on the menu!  Sorry no photos - we were really hungry.

text:
See it's kinda hard to write a review when most of your thoughts are variations on "mmmmm."

Mmmmmm...

Mmmmmm!

mmmmmMMMMM!!

mm?

MMMMMMMM!!!

That, in order, is crab/pork soup dumplings, cucumber salad, beef scallion pancakes, me wondering if I can get some more tea, and spicy beef tripe.

Everything here is executed solidly. The place is bright and punchy. You can people watch folks catching the NYC bus outside. There's little not to like. I even vaguely remember people mentioning that it's BYO, but get this - the food is so good and cheap that it doesn't even occur to me to verify this nugget of wisdom or even try to bring anything to drink.

On the real though, is this place a BYO?

text:
Jesus fucking Christ these people are slow. I can count seven employees doing various things behind the counter, not one of which is helping customers. There were two of us standing there looking at each other like, um, do we say something or just wait?
So finally the first lady puts my rice, beans and carnitas in the bowl. I asked for very little rice. She dumps a huge scoop of rice, and oh, who cares. Then I wait again. I look back and forth from one employee to the next and no one acknowledges me. 
After another awkwardly long wait, someone comes over to ask about my salsa and cheese. I get pico, a very little bit of corn salsa and sour cream. No, just a little bit of cor... Whatever. No! No cheese. No, I don't want cheese. No. No, thank you, no cheese.*all the eye rolls*
Then I'm slid down to the register to... Wait some more. Finally a cashier comes over. She gets my grapefruit izze, and, "wait, you didn't get cheese?" No. No cheese, thanks. "Oh, you don't like cheese?" Well, ma'am, I'm sure your store is OK with me getting less ingredients... what I would like is to order my bowl in less than 12 minutes next time, please.

text: OK people of Yelp: I think it's very important that if someone has a negative experience at a business and that business has tried in good faith to rectify the situation that you at least acknowledge that attempt. But more importantly that you are mature enough to let people know of that. Such is the case with Buffalo exchange my last review was pretty salty.  I was really disappointed that I was unable to purchase the items I was trying to purchase with the card that I had gotten. I can tell you that within three days of that situation the manager at Buffalo exchange had called me left voicemails and even responded to my yelp review to rectify the situation. I am late in posting this update because I've been very Busy and unable to go by there. Well I did just leave and I can tell you that the manager spoke with me, met with me, treated me with kid gloves was very nice and apologetic and empathized. I think anytime we have a negative experience at a business (I personally I can only speak for myself) I'm not looking to get something for nothing but I do want to know my feelings have been validated and the person is empathized with me. I work in a customer service job and there's many times I can't give people what they are asking for but I can absolutely relate and understand it with their position even if I don't agree with it. Major kudos to Buffalo exchange for the way they handled this, and I will continue to patronize the store (Whether they want me to or not LOL). Pardon any typos I am using voice to text because I'm a monster. Five stars because they honestly could not have handled any better.

text:
Good, consistent and super-friendly. 

This place is around the corner from our house so we have been coming here at least once a week for the past 10 years or so. The food is good -- it's not a place I would bring a first date or business client to impress them -- but if you just want a good, filling meal at a reasonable price, you can't do much better than Pete's. It's been around forever, and when you are inside you can see a microcosm of the neighborhood sitting at tables or coming in to pick up -- everyone in Logan Square goes to Pete's!

In all the years we have been coming here, we have had exactly one minor issue with the food which they quickly rectified AND took the item off the bill. Service is friendly and efficient. Beer and wine list is decent for the kind of casual place it is. Prices range from reasonable to great value. (Their happy hour in particular is dirt cheap.) Our family of three can typically get dinner (and a round or two of drinks for mom and dad) for $30-40, and still have leftovers for the next day. 

Overall a great place to pop in for a quick bite or casual meal a step above basic takeout.

text:
Very rarely do I write a review right away and very rarely do I obtain a cup of coffee that I don't have to Doctor up; yet Picasso was so excellent on the latter that I'm doing the former. 

Mid day break and needing a boost I strolled in. The lighting is very dark even during the day. There's ample seating that's not crowed and free Wi-Fi so it's the perfect place to get some work done. The pastry items are rather limited but the menu for espresso drinks is more than most coffee places and leaves one with lots of options. Whenever the option presents self, I always go with almond milk and they had it in abundance. I opted for the Mona snicker mocha with slightly less syrup than their recipe calls for.

A huge sigh of relief when I took my first sip. Yet before my first sip, the barista very pleasantly told me to let her know if I needed  any alterations. At the first sip I gave her a thumbs up and I'll I had to do was stir my warm drink. 

Picasso is centrally located within the new shopping and dining complex called The Streets of St. Charles. Right by wasabi sushi and two cute boutiques, Picasso makes it easy to grab a drink and stroll the area either while shopping, taking a break from work for a pick me up or after a workout or movie.

Whether you like hot drinks, cold drinks, bottled beverages, pastries or sandwiches; I would definitely suggest Picasso. They have winning customer service and even better offerings that'll keep anyone coming back. More so since they have a frequency rewards card and gift cards at the ready.

text:
Commander's Palace: If you're looking the epitome of a glorious icon of a place, I don't know if you can do any better. And then there's that tremendous cookin, atmosphere, and service. You should go.

It was all just straight up dreamy.

Commander's Palace is super fancy but it's not highfalutin. Top notch, professional service is guaranteed with nary a trace of snoot or stuffiness. All the staff we encountered were beyond competent and friendly t'boot. You'd be further lucky to have Lorenzo as your waiter. We were treated like royalty, like the queen and king of the thang. 

This mansion full of many gorgeous dining  rooms just oozes celebration; it's a special occasion. It certainly was that kinda lunch for us, extra so, during our visit the Monday before Christmas. A trio of carolers made their way through the dining rooms full of tourists and locals alike celebrating the season. There was just such an energetic buzz about the place. I'd go so far as to call it jubilant.  

Let's talk about those alluringly classy dining rooms, one after the next. I did not eeeeven know about the extent of the inner beauty of it all until I took the wrong staircase on our way out, after lunch. 

I somehow Mr. Magoo-ed us down to this magical courtyard of outdoor seating surrounded by Spanish moss draped oaks. Even in such a rainy day the space was gahdamn bewitching! And then there's that glass-walled dining room across the courtyard looking out over the scene. So lovely. I decided that's where I wanna be seated next time! Now that I know what goes on out in that Garden View Room...

Maybe the existence of the enchanted courtyard would not have been revealed to me, through misdirection, had it not been for those 25 cent martinis? It's possible. 

About that amazing martini deal, the menu states, "Limit three (3) per person, 'cause that's enough." Though I'm not typically the kinda ole gal who has ever come to terms with the concept of when enough's enough, maybe they're right about this... They sure went down easy with the celebratory vibe and killa cuisine. Damn that was an amazing lunch.

We started with light, fresh and flavorful Louisiana crab ravigote which was delicately dressed so as to highlight the crab, so as not to break up the lovely lumps... MmmmHM. Wonderful.

I signed on for Chef Tory MacPhail's three course price fixe, "Classic Creole Luncheon," and was certainly happy about that decision as the first course began with a bowl of the most velvety, dark-rouxed, rich gumbo. It was chicken and mushroom this time. Loved it. As much as I enjoyed several spoonful tastes of my rollin' buddy's famous, lusciously savory sherry-spiked turtle soup, I was all about that gumbo.

Second course for me was the quince glazed quail. That quince glaze was some sticky, shiny, sweet-tart deliciousness laquered onto a perfectly-cooked stuffed quail. That little baby was stuffed with cochon du lait boudin and roasted chili, by the way. Great googly moogly. Full o' flavor. It sat atop a mounded perch of brown-buttery spaghetti squash. Damn tasty. So good! Thank you lil bird n lil piggy. Thank you chef. I mean, really. That was super enjoyable. 

Tip: If you enjoy a ridiculous photo opportunity, be sure to take a picture of yourself or a pal holding that tiny quail drumstick. Caption it: World's Largest Human Enjoys Turkey Leg.

I really should mention too how much my husband enjoyed the hell outta his seafood kuby. That sauce! Smoked redfish cake... Delectable.  

Do the desserts. About the Creole bread pudding soufflé... That dish deserves all the heaps of praise that it receives. Now that's a heavenly dessert, muh people. The ethereally light soufflé scented with cinnamon, nutmeg, and vanilla is drizzled with a decadent bourbon cream sauce. You need it in your life. Need. It is excellent.

Also, the strawberry shortcake. Need. Do splitskis with your dining partner if it's on the menu. Another server raved to us about the sweet, fresh strawberries abundantly brimming on this shortcake, and the dude he was not lyin. 

You're pickin up what I'm puttin down, right? We so enjoyed every bit of our lunch at Commander's Palace. The cuisine, the gorgeous milieu, the skilled and attentive waitstaff: all five stars across the board.

 Ain't it great when a venerated institution lives up to its outstanding reputation? It's all happening here at this landmark turquoise and white striped Garden District gem of a mansion, full if classic Creole flavors.

Look sharp! Get your fancypants on and have yourself a time.

This place just doesn't do it for me. 

The pros are that the staff are very friendly and helpful and that there are fun accessories at the bar. Also it's very neat/clean inside and never busy.

The major con is basically what the place is founded on- you get someone else to get your jeans for you. No looking through different colors or feeling textures at the blue jeans bar. You describe what you want and the women behind the bar get you a pair. This works if you are totally sure of what you want. But, if you need to shop around it is the worst. 

I actually wasted a $70 groupon because I really disliked the shopping experience. I wanted a sturdy thick non-stretch jean and kept getting given the exact opposite. Maybe this works for other people...but I'm pretty bummed about that wasted money so I'd definitely go across the street to Jean Therapy.

text:
So far I really enjoy the private I have had with Trish, she is very friendly and likes to challenge you.  I've had previous experience before going to the private lesson and it is very individualized. Trish is also big on learning more static pole moves which I find is different, however necessary. 
The studio itself is a clean and bright environment. There are 4 poles 2 static and 2 on spin. Each of them 40mm. They are the skinnier pole which I'm not used to but they are in good condition. Trish keeps a few mats for safety and for floor work. My only gripe is the ceilings are not very tall so there is not room to climb higher.

I am definitely willing to come back since Trish is very personable and the price for a private is much cheaper than most studios. With military discount I was able to get the first class for $35. Additional packages are in available 5 at a time with different pricing options. 10 privates with military discount is $270, that's $27 a private! If anything just try her studio to see if it's for you.

Occupying the former Einstein Bros bagel place on the corner of the strip  of lunch spots catering to the IU medical center crowd, it shares the same small, packed parking lot, but I was glad to see that it was not very busy for lunch at 12:30 on a Monday afternoon. I ordered takeout and food was ready by the time I got out of the bathroom. 
I have no complaints about the food, would've given three stars, but added a star for having sushi and bubble tea. I ordered the Kung Pao chicken Bento box. Good ratio of chicken - veggies, even threw in some asparagus, not too spicy, plain fried rice was good, about 1/2 quantity to entree. Four pieces of California roll were fine, with plenty of ginger and wasabi. Nice to see they threw in the low sodium soy sauce automatically. Cream cheese Rangoon and egg roll were hot and crispy, but not much substance to them. Mango bubble tea was a bit too sweet for me, probably contains some premade mixture, but tapioca balls were soft. will definitely be back to try the sushi-can't wait till this pregnancy is over!

text: 12th and porter is one of the many great little music venues in Nashville. Inside you'll find two different rooms with stages, of which the bigger one is definitely the better - not only those it have excellent acoustic, but the upstairs balcony and bar are definitely a great surprise, and fantastic place to watch the show. Sadly, however, I always have go back to my annoyance with ridiculous amounts of smoke. I don't know if this has changed recently with the new sweep of smoke-free zones in Nashville (for which I'm eternally thankful) but I remember the bar in the main area being really smoky. Also, during the summer 12th and Porter shows get uncomfortably hot in the hideous mid-south heat. All in all I think this is a good place to catch a show in the bigger room, especially if you snag a spot in the upstairs balcony!

text: My boyfriend and I have been looking for a certified used Jeep Grand Cherokee for several months now and happened to find exactly what we were looking for at this dealership. He stopped in once to check it out himself, then we returned together for 4 grueling hours of trying to come to an agreement with the help of the salespeople. Alex S. and John were excellent, honest, and really hoping to help us come to a complete sale. However, when John couldn't sell us on the "deal" we were being offered ($50/mo. more than what we budgeted), he brought in the "big dawgs" AKA an angry middle-aged Eastern European guy who would have promised us the moon if we had asked for it. He was terribly condescending, pushy, and dishonest. "We'll offer you no payments until Sept." he told us (later learning it's industry standard that payments don't start for 45 days). "We'll give you free inspection and tank of gas" (also industry standard). He would barely allow us time to sit, think, and discuss if we could swing the higher cost, which we were prepared to do. He made us feel guilty that we were wasting their time and that because of us, he wouldn't receive his bonus for the month. We somehow managed to leave the building requesting the weekend to think on our decision (this was Saturday evening). We returned first thing Monday morning, as promised, to complete the purchase. What we encountered, once again, was being tossed from nice salesperson to pushy sales manager repeatedly and feeling very uncomfortable with the whole car buying process. We had a horrible feeling about this place, decided to walk away, and were badgered by not one, but two angry dudes prior to leaving telling us "Are you fucking kidding me? You came in here 3 times! This is bullshit! John didn't do his job! This is fucking ridiculous!" Are you kidding me? This is your job, sir. I am not here to help you meet your quotas or bonuses. Frankly, I don't give a shit about your bonus and in fact, you should be trying to provide your customers with confidence and service to help them purchase a $40,000 vehicle and feel good about it. I shouldn't be here boosting your ego and worrying about your wallet. So, guess what? We purchased the same damn vehicle from a VERY nice gentleman at Reedman Toll who made the entire process so simple. We were treated fairly, respected, and not pushed or fed a bunch of BS along the way.
