text:
Some might immediately say, "5 Stars?" 

*What about the 600 million dollar bill they left the tax payers to foot? Answer: blame the city. 

*What about cutting Peyton Manning? Answer: what about Peyton Manning offering to restructure his deal since he was paid 26 million for the season he didn't play? 

*What about cutting all those fan favorite players today? Answer: a sign of commitment to recreating an organization from top to bottom to craft a new culture. 

This past year has been a roller coaster for Colts fans -- tack on the fact we hosted the Super Bowl to Manning's brother & Tom Brady. 

The Colts will be around long after Peyton Manning, and the Colts were around long before Manning. Duane Bickett. Jack Trudeau. Jeff George (yeaaaaa!). Harbaugh! Aaron Bailey & his almost perfect catch. Siragusa. Billy Brooks. Dickerson. Poole. Ken Dilger. Clarence Verdin. Sean Dawkins. Trev Alberts (Nebraska!). Roosevelt Potts. Roosevelt Colvin! LaMont Warren. And, Quentin Coryatt. And that's not even talking about the Baltimore era. 

These are the players I grew up loving to watch. There are going to be a litany of players again in this generation. And better yet... for the first time... the entire organization is on the same page about the culture they want to build from the very beginning. 

Think about the decisions this team has had to make and every single time they've come out on top: 

*Baltimore or Indy? 
*Manning or Leaf?
*Too give everything to keep Dungy or not?
*Polian or a new era?
*Manning or a new era? 

Each time they made the right choice. At this point... Irsay has built my trust. At this point... you might be wondering why I'm so positive during "The Week the Colts lost Manning." Well... he lost us. He lost the Colts. And that's why he was so sad. And, if you ever want to broach the topic of why I don't agree that he's the greatest of all time or even that I like him, by all means... broach. Also go ahead and broach the topic of how I don't like Tony Dungy, too. 

What do I like? I love the Colts, but I'm picky -- very picky -- just as much, if not more, as what I eat & drink. If I'm going to show my love for a team (meaning: pay arms, legs & take from my razor fund for games & take away from my time on other awesome things on learning the genius of coffee) I'm going to want them to be exactly the way I want them to be. I've wanted a hard-nosed, brittle, defensive minded coach and young, aggressive GM since I started being a Colts fan as a kid. And now I have that, so my personal experience with this team right is through the roof. Plus, our owner is awesome. Across the street from his team's stadium he has his personal oddity collection on display at the State Museum, which includes the original scroll of Keruoac's On The Road. 

This past week I heard an amazing quote: "In the Bronx Tale, Sammy says, "hey, consider yourself lucky if you have three great women in your life. That's just the same as a Colts fan." Unitas. Manning. Luck. 

And, what are the chances that a team with a horseshoe on their helmet would have a franchise QB named "Luck?" It's true... we're a lucky bunch.

text: It was great to see one of my favorite pizza places busier than I've seen since COVID started. For the first time ever I was a little disappointed in the mushrooms, they were left in the fryer a little too long but the pizza was cooked to perfection. text:
I stopped by here to grab lunch on a weekend without a reservation. We had a table of 3, and I believe our servers name was Daniel. There was a hostess in training, so we were sat a bit late and the trainer looked a bit flustered.

I ordered a sangria and the server failed to tell me for 2 dollars more I could've had a pitcher, which would have been nice to know to share with the table. We found out afterwards which was kind of a bummer. It was on the menu, so I should've seen it myself, oops. We will remember for next time. But he was so quick to recommend dishes and drinks (which we ordered with his advice)!

The service was good and the food was excellent. We dine at Carrabba's around 3-4 times a month and this location, hands down, had the best quality of food.

The Tuscan strawberry salad is the best salad I have ever had, even with no meat added. I wish they would expand their menu and add more salads or more vegan/vegetarian sauces. The others at the table ordered seafood pasta dishes which they said was more than enough mussels and shrimp on their plate. 

The food is very delicious and the atmosphere is great. The only downside was our service was a bit off, but I understand that those things happen, especially in the service industry!

text:
Riverbend Cycles is a wonderful and much needed addition to Conshohocken.  

My husband and I bought this busted up old tandem bike and were debating where we should take it to get it restored into it's original glory.  We took the bike in to Riverbend and the guys there were knowledgeable, welcoming, and fun.  They were able to totally restore our bike, which needed a number of specialty parts being old and tandem, in just three days and for a VERY reasonable price to its original glory.   They laid out some options for us as well so that we could choose what price point of restoration we wanted.  This was much appreciated especially when most of the bike places in philly will just rip you off!  

Riverbend also has bike rentals when your non-bike owning friends come into town and want to go for a ride.  They also sell bikes, clothes, parts, etc.   Bonus, they have a cafe connected to the shop.  We didn't go over there but if it's anything like the actual shop, I'm sure it's perfect. 

Absolutely recommend stopping in here if you live in the area or popping in to check it out if you're taking a trip on the river trail.  This and the conshohocken brewery are the types of places Conshy needs!!   It's as good or better than any bike place in Philly because you don't have to deal with the price tag or the snobby bike attitudes.

text:
What has happened to Kohl's?...used to be one of my favorite stores. Arrived here this evening to look for a purse. The place was empty...only a handful of customers were in the store.  Employees were milling around, but no one bothered to ask customers if they needed any help.  Went to the handbag section and the place was a mess. Stuff in the aisles...and the purses were all disheveled. It looked like a thrift store.  I went to the front counter to ask a question and the girl just pointed me back to where I came from. Asked another employee and she acted as if I was annoying her and tried to get away as fast as possible. Saw a couple of managers chatting and another trying to avoid eye contact in case he should have to answer a question.
How sad. This once innovative store looks like a shadow of its former self. When you have more employees than customers in the store, it should be a wake-up call.

text:
New Day Gluten Free is a true sanctuary for those of us who can't process wheat like the rest of you lucky folks. There's even a sign on the door stating no outside food can be brought in, to ensure the environment isn't contaminated!

Indeed, New Day is 100% wheat-free. Visiting their new location had topped my bucket list for the last couple of months, as they were moving their previous shop to a shiny, larger location in Clayton. And what a gorgeous space it is! Just going on 3 weeks new, we snatched the last open table on a busy Saturday.

Nothing can prepare you for the beauty that lies in the glass-encased baked good display case. Generously frosted cupcakes, gooey butter cookies, lemon bars, the cutest cakes and much more! I ordered a carrot cupcake and lemon bar to-go for my mom and me, and for lunch we opted for a turkey club and a California melt.

While both sandwiches were outstanding, I think my mom was a bit envious of the warm bun that hugged the overflowing toppings of my sandwich. Fresh turkey, provolone cheese, a liberal spread of guacamole, onions, lettuce and some mayo. But THAT BREAD! Wow. Just as good as bread made with conventional flour. This was my favorite type of sandwich in my gluten-eating days, so it was a dream to take in. 

After suppressing the temptation for a solid 8 hours, I finally gave in to the decadent carrot cupcake that had been staring me down all day. Holy cream cheese frosting! That was definitely the highlight for me and then some, as the cake portion was slightly dry. Still, it was one of the better gluten-free desserts I've had. Next time, I'll be trying the vanilla cupcake as that's my usual go-to treat.

Thanks for filling in a much-needed void in St. Louis, New Day! You've earned some new lifetime fans.

text: I hate to do this but I have to call them out on this. DON'T BUY THEIR MINESTRONE SOUP. Last night I bought their Chicken and Wild rice Soup with some deli protein and everything was 5 star. The soup was awesome. Tonight I went in and decided to try the Minestrone. As I write this I still can't believe they put this product out. Minestrone is as Italian as it gets. text:
I recently purchased a pair of Frye boots at Winners... for $40!!!! The catch was that the back zipper was broken and needed replacing. I was a little weary of purchasing something that was already in need of repairs... but I knew their original value (~$500) and made sure that I was able to return them to Winners within a week if I couldn't find a repair shop. 

Anyways, I took them into Downtown Shoe Repair and the guy takes one look and says $50 (which is about what I expected... taking out and putting in a new zipper is a ton of work!). And then asks me how much I paid for them and wouldn't stop talking about what an amazing deal I got! (It made me feel better knowing a shoe repair guy thought it was worth it, haha). He pulled a couple zippers from the back and made sure I approved of the color. And then asked me if tomorrow was okay (he actually could have had them ready same day!). No problemo. It was a bit informal... I didn't get a ticket saying which shoes were mine or any receipt at all that I'd dropped from off. When I asked he said to just say I was the one with the zipper. I was a bit hesitant but figured the good reviews meant it was okay.

And it was! I went back the next day and picked up my like new boots. He did a fantastic job putting the zipper in and you honestly wouldn't be able to tell which boot was repaired if you didn't ask or look super closely. They fit perfectly and are already my new favorite boots! 

Would absolutely recommend. I'll be taking all my shoe repairs here from now on.

text:
I'm from the St. Louis area originally. So of course, for 21 years this was the airport that I had to use if I ever wanted to go anywhere. 
Since the fall of TWA, this airport has become fairly empty (even though AA has a pretty big hub and Southwest basically has it's own terminal...)

For such an empty airport, the security lines are RIDICULOUS in the Main Concourse (AA, Delta, etc.) And the "security" at this airport seem to be worse than they are at others, as far as they will pat you down if you're wearing a cardigan sweater and refuse to take it off. I'm not kidding. I always dress in layers when I travel (usually including a cardigan sweater) and I've NEVER been asked to take it off or been patted down anywhere else. Oh, and heaven forbid you ask any of them a question! They'll look at you like you've got 12 heads. And then they'll proceed to get to their golf cart and joyride around the practically empty terminal. Seriously. 

However, the East Terminal (Southwest) is a huge improvement. The lines are reasonable and the girls who work at the Starbucks (which I'm pretty sure is the only food/drink in the terminal) are very nice. So lucky you if you fly on Southwest out of St. Louis! (or Frontier.... I think that's the other airline that uses the East Terminal...)

text:
I work on South Grand so I might be a little biased... But here's my take on Mojo Tapas Restaurant & Bar:

It's nice to have a tapas restaurant that not over-the-top pricey and has accessible food both in price and flavor. It's even nicer to have a place like Mojo in my own neighborhood.

I love how the tapas menu touches on a variety of flavor palettes, with the dishes just large enough for a small group to taste.  When I visited, we sampled the roasted pork empanadas, lamb sliders, sweet potato/andouille hash, seared ahi tuna, edamame hummus, and shrimp & "sexy gritz".

I could elaborate on each dish, and tell you how tasty they were, but that should go without saying... It's all delicous! I highly suggest the "Sexy Gritz" and any of their empanadas. After another visit, I also suggest the veggie cakes and smoked trout salad: awesome!

Our sampling, plus drinks and dessert (Banana brioche bread pudding and apple turnover w/ vanilla ice cream!!!) our bill was about $70. Not bad for a tapas dinner for three.

Our service was very helpful and knowledgeable of the menu; which is nice to see in a restaurant in its opening stages. Just remember to let the bussers know that you're not finished with that last bite of hummus, they might be a little aggressive.

Again, it's such a pleasure to be in such close proximity to another restaurant that cares about its food, its customers, and its neighborhood.

text: Hubby brought me here on my birthday, and we tried 3 different flavors. Carrot, German chocolate, and chocolate chocolate chocolate. First thought was that it's pretty pricey for a slice of cake, but it is a decent size. Employee let us know to let it come to room temperature for at least 15-20 mins before wanting to eat it. We waited about 30 mins, and tried the carrot cake first. I was very disappointed with that, as carrot cake is my favorite kind of cake. I was expecting the usual cream cheese frosting, but it was just a regular frosting and the cake itself was very bland and dry. The German chocolate was a little better flavor wise but still dry. The chocolate chocolate chocolate was the same. Flavor was alright but dry as well. Not a fan and don't understand the hype of this place. Raley's make better cakes and they're half the price. So disappointed, especially since cakes are their specialty here.

text:
Great place! Located in bustling St. Pete, it's a step away into a quiet and colorful jungle of plant and wildlife. The place is full of history, being built over 100 years ago in a former sinkhole. At one point it was run down but they've done a great job working to restore it to its former glory. You can bring your family and a picnic lunch and enjoy the grounds. They even do (very expensive) weddings and private events here!

We found a deal on Groupon for a family annual pass to Sunken Gardens, which seemed to be a pretty good deal compared to daily admission (around $30). When we arrived, it was easy to park (free) and find the entrance (inside a building that looks like a gift shop). We found that with our membership, we can get into many botanical gardens around the US, including Bok Tower Gardens in Lake Wales. We also get $4 off per person at the Armed Forces Museum, and we get half price admission at the Florida Aquarium. Great deal! 

We spent about an hour or so here (it was getting hot), and the my husband and kids really enjoyed our visit. Make sure to grab some Koi food for $1 at the entrance stand, as there are many koi ponds throughout the gardens. We saw lots of beautiful plant life such as orchids, bougainvillea, trees, flowering plants, succulents, etc. In addition to the beautiful winding passages and the plants, they have flamingos, tortoises, koi fish, parrots and parakeets, macaws, and visiting squirrels. The birds were pretty funny and they'd "talk" to you as you checked them out. 

Our only complaint was that we wished more of the plants and things were labeled. Some sections were, but it was hard to follow the map they give you when nothing is labeled the way it is on the map. We can't wait to go back!

text:
My friend is on a mission to try all of the vegetarian and vegan restaurants around Tampa Bay. For her birthday lunch, we visited 3 Dot Dash. Located inside Jug and Bottle, 3 Dot Dash offers vegan fare available through pick-up, delivery, or on-site patio dining. I happened to have brought my own water to drink, but since it's inside a beverage and beer store, there's no shortage of great brews to pair your meal with.

I ordered the Rodeo Buster. It consists of a 1/4 lb Impossible burger, house made onion rings, red BBQ sauce, Follow Your Heart American cheese, and a pickle. The burger is huge with big flavor to match. I loved the sauce and the crispy onion rings. I was very happy with my faux burger.

I ordered a side of the vegan Mac, and it was my FAVORITE part of my meal! I am usually not a fan of vegan "cheese" but this one hit the flavor right out of the park! The Mac consists of elbow macaroni baked southern style in cashew "cheese" sauce. I truly regret not ordering a second helping of Mac to go.

There is not a lot of parking in the dedicated lot for Jug and Bottle. During rush hour, it could get tricky finding parking along the street. The building has a large patio area for eating with long picnic tables to sit at. 

I am so glad I got to try 3 Dot Dash. I'll be dreaming of the Mac until I'm in the area to order it again.

text:
If I want a burger, Fharmacy. If someone asks me for burger suggestions, I tell them Fharmacy without thinking twice. There is no competition in this city. I mean it. They grind their own meat and form the patties themselves. They know the real definition of medium and medium rare. The burger can stand alone but the toppings really take it to another level. 

If burgers aren't your thing order the dragon wings. I dip my fries in the sauce and I dare someone to judge me. I can't even describe the flavor because I don't want to do it a disservice. I also enjoy the mussels and frites. 

You can tell this family really cares about their food and they have put a lot of thought in to what they want to offer. You will be greeted by and taken care of by the owners and their family members. 

I don't know how they do it. Everything is always perfect. Always.

text:
In a really tough neighbourhood for coffee, Transcend is a quality presence.  The espresso here is really really good.  That says a lot when there are at least five quality baristas slinging brew in easy walking distance.  I go to Europe quite a bit and but for places like Transcend, I won't be able to drink coffee back home.  Certainly that bitter swill that Starbucks produces in their campaign to destroy good coffee can't bring me down from a week in coffee heaven. In short, if you like coffee, you will not be disappointed here.

If there are cons to this location, they're that there is no decent outdoor seating area which is just a must-have in the Summer.  Also, the Transcend vibe can attract some real "coffee snobs" who're a little too concerned about how they look and what they order than having a truly good time or sense of humour.  I tend to take mine to go when the atmosphere is thick with the "intelligensia".

biz_attributes_clean: GoodForKids: True, DogsAllowed: False, BikeParking: True, ByAppointmentOnly: False, BusinessParking: {'garage': False, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': True, 'valet': False}
text:
For a culturally-immersive, physically-demanding and uniquely-Tucson experience that is being described by a record number of barely-passable, impossibly-hyphenated adjectives as you are witnessing in this review, there is no better outdoor activity than stumbling up Tumamoc Hill while sober. Located west of the I-10, the top of the hill affords 360 views of downtown Tucson, the mountains surrounding Tucson and beyond. But now I'm getting ahead of myself, and pacing is important on this hill.

The walk is fairly short in mileage - about 3 miles roundtrip. But the elevation gain is 700 feet, most of which is made at the second half of the journey, whose beginning is marked by the two decently-maintained porta potties. U of A owns this piece of greatness, which is adjacent to a hospital and close to downtown Tucson. 

After many years of restrictive access to the Hill, the U of A has now opened the area during daylight hours everyday and has also repaved the road to save us from ankle-sprain angst. There are several benches along the way, usually marked with signs about the flor and fauna in the area.

Despite the grueling climb, we now bring all of our out-of-town guests to Tumamoc. Besides ensuring that our guests will be too tired to do anything else for the duration of their stay with us - thereby relieving us of our hosting duties - a hike on the hill is the full-on experience for the senses: combining the beautiful Tucson scenery with a great introduction to the many peoples and cultures of Tucson, as well as the sense that we are inferior physical specimens of what human beings should be.

Here, we will find the lady pushing her quintuplets in a baby stroller as she runs effortlessly up the hill without breaking a sweat. (The quintuplets are probably doing bicep curls with their baby bottles as she runs.) The gladiator man, who is not only wearing a weighted vest, but is also hauling a bag of cement on his back...while running backwards.

But there are also people here who would otherwise never participate in outdoor physical activity. We have seen families with toddlers who have walked the hill (with much encouragement), very elderly people who have hiked up with canes - you name it. There's also an app about Tumamoc and its history, which you can listen to as you plod your way up. A truly iconic Tucson experience!

text:
You better make a beeline for Beline! I had been hearing good things about this place and put it on my "To Visit" list, so when my wife said we needed to head to Lemmon Valley for an errand, I decreed that we had to stop by and check Beline out.
     The store itself is small, clean, and colorful. There's a meat counter, a few coolers/freezers, and some shelves with lots of Mexican sweets and snacks. The lady working the counter greeted us upon arrival and gave us a quick rundown of the menu, which of course is very torta-oriented. My wife ordered a torta with al pastor and I went for the birria torta.
     There is nowhere to eat inside but there are a few tables outside. It was getting dark and chilly so we opted to dine in our vehicle. The friendly counter lady offered to bring our food out to our car when it was ready.
     We received our food about five minutes later and dug in. My birria torta was fantastic! The beef was very well-flavored with hints of clove and other spices. The broth was incredible! Even after I had finished my torta I had to sip the rest of it. I would drink that stuff straight from a dirty boot! My wife's al pastor torta was also excellent, good flavor and just enough heat. The salsas included were also great. I had to finish them with some tortilla chips because I didn't want any to go to waste.
     Beline Carniceria is a hit in my book and will be a preferred torta destination for us as opposed to a place to hit up when we are in the area.

text: Yea its not the best ball park anymore (was at one time) in the majors but its still pretty damn good! Old school charm mixed with modern amenities. Best spot to see a sporting event in Philly by far! Easy, but pricey parking, and usually traffic waits to get in and out are tolerable. Staff that works there are usually courteous. (Just wish they would be a little faster to get rid of the foul mouth drunks at times) Plenty of great food options, although some might suggest pricey? Try McFadden's just outside the park or Tony Luke's, Bull's Barbecue inside, or maybe even Harry the K's for nicer venue inside. I find that no matter where you sit (except in a couple spots) are good seats. Especially worth the money are Hall of Fame seats, if you can get them for good price (under 40 bucks a pop) Some even don't mind the standing room only just to be there, but the place is half empty now, so they you can usually get seats cheap in the upper decks. Shame the product that plays there stinks, but once they start playing winning ball again, I expect this place to be rockin, and once again be the place to be seen on those hot Philly Summer evenings!

text:
I could distill this review to two words:

Roast Pork

But then I would be overlooking the great yellow rice and beans, and the friendly owners and staff that cook up that pork with the most incredible marinade I've ever tasted anywhere.  Just a perfect balance of flavor, herb and spice.  Indescribably delicious.  I talked to the owner so long about how much great it was, he brought out the container he mixes the marinade in just to let me take a look at it and have a sniff.  

I haven't tried anything else on the menu because I haven't eaten enough roast pork here before I die.  And without question Tia's passes that critical measure of any great family restaurant/diner.....the cop test.  I counted 6 there during a recent lunch, with 2 more that stopped by for takeout.

There are not a lot of places to get Latin flavors in Largo, so it's great to have Tia's to save trips all the way to Tampa.

Atmosphere:  3

Food:   5
Value:   5

text:
I came here with my friend and her Singaporean family, which has a culture that's similar to the medley reflected in the menu. I would have never ventured to order some of the options that her mom helped us pick. 

We got some classic dishes such as Penang's famous roti, of course. The pastry came out piping hot, but we couldn't resist diving in and tearing it with our hands. 

One of my favorite dishes ended up being the oyster omelet. Sounded strange at first, but the sauce that it came with was also good. I learned... when asked white rice or chicken rice, I now recommend going for the latter. It's cooked with chicken broth and is so much more flavorful! We paired it with roast chicken and greens... a classic and delightful entree. 

Unfortunately, the tom yum noodles was not very good. The soup itself lacked depth. The flavor was too sour (tasted a little artificial) and not fragrant or spicy at all. Fresh lemongrass flavors are the best part about it, and we really missed those notes.

As I, we, were coming down the escalator I asked My sister, how did Kate Spade get so popular, her line is so pedestrian. Now mind you, I am a customer talking softly to my sister and as we were passing the hand bags I hear this very loud voice of a male saying, and we just started caring Kate Spade. I know this is reno people still think Michael Kors is fashionable while the rest of the country has moved on. As he moved on in his loud voice he continued to speak ill of the taste of the reno customer. This guy was short, or maybe not as the woman with him was a tall very thin blonde. In any case he wore glasses and an ill fitting suit. I assume he works for Dillard's.

So dude, yeah I agree your merchandise is lacking in any fashion foreword thinking but that is your buyers fault, not the customer. The customer buys what is available even if it is butt ugly. I can say these things to my sister, you should not be broadcasting it to your buying customers. 

And still I like Dillard's

Really?  An ice cream sandwich shop? I thought it was kind of niche at first but since it's a build your own type thing you can deconstruct it however you want. Just cookies? Sure. Ice cream only? Yep. Brownies too. The best thing is that you can mix and match it up however you want. I asked the person behind the counter any kind of combination I could think of before I decided on the plain ol' ice cream sandwich. 

Now, there are some other places that offer an ice cream sandwich option but not to the extent here. The ice cream options here are way more varied. I ended up getting the blueberry crumble, with a snickerdoodle cookie top and chocolate chip cookie bottom. You can also get your choice of toppings on it and pressed. I felt toppings were excessive, if I was in the mood for an ice cream cookie sandwich sundae, and I do feel like one now that I've typed those words you could. Although, a pseudo-panini pressed ice cream sandwich sounds weird, she swore by it I wasn't sure if heat+ice cream was a good combo.

If you love American cereals, chocolate bars, candy, cookies, chips, and other treats, you'll want to check this place out. If you're ever complaining that America get's better flavours of oreos than us, just go here. And if you're into fitness and all about those macros, you'll definitely find some special treats here from protein cookies and butters, to protein powders. 

Be warned, you are not paying normal grocery store prices. Expect to pay at least double whatever it would sell for in the US. Keep in mind, these guys have to pay exchange rates and shipping costs, and keep their lights on. So plan to spend a pretty coin here, but it's totally worth it!

I also highly recommend following them on instagram as they are always posting their latest stock @johnnybsweet.

Monte Cristo Sandwich -Battered and Fried French Toast with Ham, Swiss, Raspberry Puree
Aebleskivers with Nutella and Lemon Curd

A "locals-only" sort of place, even in a city where many eateries outside The French Quarter have justifiably achieved recognition by way of culinary tourism, Toast in Uptown proved to be a every bit as good as its neighbors will tell you during a Wednesday morning breakfast at the counter, every single bite delicious from the first of a warm Muffin to the final fluffy 'Pancake.'

Located at 5433 Laurel Street and open from 6:30am until 2:00pm Monday through Friday while sleeping in until eight o'clock on weekends, Toast does not look like much from the shabby façade to the interior housing little more than a kitchen, wooden bar and several tightly-packed low tops, the team and its Chefs instead allowing the wall-mounted menu to do the talking with a wide range of Eggs, Skillets, Crepes and more.

Served by two young ladies as a trio of men worked with gusto behind the stoves, it was with a few daily specials presented that the meal was arranged, the aforementioned Caramel Apple Muffin plucked from a tray and presented with a texture not unlike that of Sticky Toffee Pudding while the Cinnamon Roll offered up a tight coil packed with Fruit and Nuts beneath tangy Cream Cheese Frosting.

One of the rare places stateside to offer Danish Pancakes, full or half-orders presented with a choice of Sauce and additional cups provided at fifty-cents each, Toast's Aebleskivers are perfectly formed orbs straight from the pan that pair well with Nutella but better with housemade Lemon Curd, the same made-from-scratch approach taken for Raspberry Puree presented alongside a world-class Monte Cristo densely packed with local Ham while the "King Cake" Crepe presents the thin French Pancake folded over sweetened Cream beneath colorful sugar granules partially melted by Praline Syrup.

text:
Hash House A Go Go is not going to be your go to brunch spot week after week, but it is the kind of restaurant that you bring someone to that's visiting out of town. Or maybe your city friends decide to trek over to the suburbs to meet you for a meal and you want them to experience a place unlike any other. 

Came here for Sunday brunch with a party of 7 at Noon. I am a sucker for comfort food. Whenever I look at a menu and have a hard time deciding what to order, its a good sign. I ended up with the sausage biscuit gravy with griddled mashed potatoes. Others in my party ordered the meatloaf, the chicken and waffles, the lemon curd pancake, and the spicy chicken sandwich. I would recommend you split a dish with someone else if you're a light eater or be prepared to walked out with leftovers. The portions are HUGE. Come hungry, make sure it's a cheat day, and enjoy!

i remember thinking "a 'container store'? the hell is this supposed to be?" when it was announced then filed it away in the recycle bin of my brain. cue forward quite a few months, and i have a reason to go here. i'll be moving to a place soon (fingers crossed, yelpketeers) that's smaller than the place i'm at now by about 350 sq feet, though has a huge closet. 

but i'm a man.

i really didn't care about the closet size until i started thinking about the stuff that i need to take with me that i need to stow away, then i really cared about that huge closet. i started doing some research on how to maximize that space and ran into this store. a woman noticed me bumbling around the store and went to help me/see what the hell my problem was. i told her what i needed and we were able to design a freestanding shelving system that will be able to store what i need for the kitchen and gave me plenty of ideas for the big bedroom closet.

for those of you that live in a smaller space and need to maximize that smaller footprint, you can find lots of quality stuff here that'll probably help you out. there is a ton of other stuff for the SUPER organizers (not me).

great customer service, they have a good thing going here.

eric b

biz_attributes_clean: GoodForKids: True, WheelchairAccessible: False, WiFi: 'no', BikeParking: True, BusinessParking: {'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': True, 'valet': False}
text:
If you drive down the strip in Tarpon Springs where the sponge docks are, you'll see many dolphin cruise places, but I don't see how the experience could be any better than the trip we took. 

Booking was somewhat tricky...I tried to book online but after going through the whole booking process (including entering my cc info) I got an error message that said "we're having trouble with this reservation, please call (727) 934-0547 to complete the reservation". So I called and Heather told me that only 3 people had signed up and they require at least 12 people per cruise so she really wasn't sure if the boat ride would be happening or not. She said she would call me and let me know either way. So we headed to Tarpon Springs planning to shop and eat and not really knowing if this boat ride was going to happen. We had just sit down to eat when she called and said a group of 14 signed up and we told her we'd be right there!

 Visited on July 6, 2020 for the 4:30pm cruise.  They sell drink tickets for beer, wine, soda, chips, margs, and tropical drinks.  They do have a bathroom on the boat so drink up. $18.99 for adults and $9.99 for children. They have you wear a mask at the boarding area but it's optional on the boat. Social distancing was arranged between families on the boat. 

It was a 2 hour boat ride down the Anclote River to the gulf of Mexico. Along the way, the tour guide pointed out the Anclote lighthouse, and told us all about pirates, soldiers, and native american exploits in the area. We stopped halfway to get out onto this little beach that had all kinds of seashells! They give you a little net bag to collect shells and once you get back to the boat they give you a brown paper bag to put your shells into and you give them back their net bag. You can take the shells home! 

On the way back our tour guide pointed out a little yellow house and said it was part of a movie filming once. The name of the movie is "Beneath The 12 Mile Reef" (1953) featuring Robert Wagner. How fitting that they mention him on a boat ride LOL (all you young people can go google how Natalie Wood died) The movie is about the cut-throat competitive world of sponge fishing in Tarpon Springs. We watched it that night and it had amazing underwater footage, old timey scuba suits, and you get to watch Gilbert Roland kick several asses. 

A fantastic tour, friendly staff, clean boat, and a great adventure for cheap. I highly recommend this company.

text:
I discover this place on a first date. The girl and I didn't last, but I've been going to the place for over 7 years now.   This is the best Mexican food in the Philadelphia area. 

Finally, someone in Philadelphia that  cooks my favorite  Mexican dish: carnitas. The carnitas are great with the pork pieces well marinated and cooked. I went regularly for years to get their carnitas. 

But I put on a lot of weight from the pork. I recently lost a lot of weight and my stomach was too tiny to eat all that pork I eat in the past, so I have a new dish enchiladas verde that I eat. The enchiladas verde  are chicken enchiladas in green sauce and good as the carnitas were.

The review has been long overdue, but a reason I was slow on it was business was always great, all the time I was there didn't seem like the need for my help. Even during the pandemic before a vaccine arrive, I had to wait for 30 minutes to get service, so that should tell you something about the quality of the food. But today, I finally gotten to its review and if this review brings a new person in to try it, I am sure that person will thank me for encouraging them to try Las Margaritas.

The peppery sauce was just more satisfying. But what all the chickens had in common was the juicy meat and the light, crispy skin! So good! The radish and coleslaw help cut some of the greasiness away. 
The ddeokbokki was fine. Nice and spicy with a fun chewiness of the rice cakes. But I would stick with the chicken on my next order. 
Overall, I decided that I don't need to go deep into Philadelphia to get KFC.

text:
Northern Liberities finally has a brewery again (RIP Schmidt's). I've seen the beginnings of this brewery since late last year and have been patiently waiting for it's arrival. This brewery is perfectly themed for the area with a good mix of indoor and outdoor seating (firepits!) serving a classic - beer and pizza.

I have tried almost all the beers (not really an IPA person or a stout person) and I have really enjoyed them all. The smoky vanilla porter was a stand out in their selection but I think my ride or die will be the Wildey Wheat. It's such an easy beer to drink. The pizza on the other hand is TOP NOTCH. I love a chewy crust that has a little umph to it. My favorite so far is the Rita, which is just a classic margarita pizza. Just thinking about it makes me want to stop by for happy hour...which is awesome. $10 Rita's and $3 8oz pours. I like that they do their 8oz's on happy hour so you can try a bunch of brews and find your favorite. I think they have a few other pizza's at the $10 happy hour price but Rita is my homegirl. I was impressed that they even have a vegan (no cheese) pizza and other vegan options.

Staff is a little squeaky but that's what you get going to a brand new place. It's worth the visit (plus free parking in the lot next door!). You have no excuse to not make this your next Philly beer excursion.

categories: Cinema, Arts & Entertainment
biz_attributes_clean: ByAppointmentOnly: False, DogsAllowed: False, WiFi: u'free', GoodForKids: True
text:
1st time here and was very positive. Went with a friend to see Wonder Woman and was very surprised at the entry way and gastro pub as soon as you enter. I know the theater is fairly new but the place was still spotless. The staff were very cordial and expedited the process for getting tickets. We had sometime to kill as we arrived early so decided to get a drink at the bar. The bartender was new but she was amazing. She was super friendly and explained all about the drinks we asked. She made recommendations which were very god. They allow you to take drinks into the theater and place an wristband on you. I've never had movie theater popcorn with a passion punch before...lol. Not bad!

The space in the theater was unbelievable. I am used to being crammed in seat like sardines and little if any rom to even adjust your position. These had electronically controlled recliners which were extremely comfortable. My only comment is as always the theater was freezing. I chose to wear long pants and a sweater but others had blankets with them. I will certainly take my "blanky" the next time as well.

great movie, excellent, ambiance, superb customer service. Highly recommend this place for an evening out. Everything in one place.

text:
Dined here last night with my boyfriend for restaurant week. I ordered the clam chowder, crab and shrimp stuffed salmon and vanilla bean creme brûlée. The menu said the chowder was award winning, but I thought it was just okay. My salmon was absolutely delicious and I loved all the flavors that went into it. The vanilla bean was also pretty good. My bf ordered the crispy coconut shrimp, parmesan crusted flounder and peanut butter pie. His shrimp was awful. I'm sorry but it was over cooked and shriveled up. Did not taste like shrimp, no hints of coconut and we both agreed that if we were blind folded we would not have been able to guess what we were eating. It sadly just tasted burned. His flounder and peanut butter pie were both outstanding 10/10. Out of everything we ordered, I'd recommend the flounder, salmon, and peanut butter pie! 

Service was also just okay. Our waiter was very friendly, but we had to wait a very long time for water, to order drinks, and to order food. This sounds like a minor complaint, but they forgot to give us our basket of bread. Tables all around us were getting bread and refills of it, but not us so we had to ask for some. Very minor, but a bit off and annoying that we had to ask for it. 

Beautiful establishment in a great location. Our experience was not the best last night, but that doesn't mean I wouldn't come back again. Maybe it was just a one time experience. I would return and reorder the flounder, pie and maybe some steak next time!

text:
So lets face it, Philly is not New York and you can run out of new places to go to on a weekend night pretty often.  So trying a not-so-hip or trendy place will happen every now and then just so you can break your routine.  That being said, me and the crew trekked to Fat Tuesdays on a Saturday night to see what it was about.  

Another reason I came here was because my friend said it was a $5 cover all night long, (not bad...and I wouldn't have to rush to get to a place before 11pm, so sounded okay).  Well things have changed.  It's now a $10 cover after 11, and of course I got there at like 12 :-(  I really hate paying cover...especially for a place like this.  

When I arrived, it reminded me of a bar in the islands with it's cheesy island decor and tropical frozen drinks. I tried the octane drink with the extra shot since this was supposedly the "thing" to do and the strongest drink.  It was a nice refreshing frozen drink that tasted like Tang. 

Fat Tuesday also has a new policy where they keep all the lights on at all times to avoid fights (apparently the bouncer had a bottle smashed over his head recently-ouch!).  But hey, at least you'll know what that guy/girl looks like in the daylight, right? Your beer goggles would have to be super duper strong for you to make a mistake here.  

The DJ was not memorable at all.  

The crowd seemed typical for this type of place.  Younger girls who seemed slightly easy and older men who were more than willing to spend their paycheck on rounds of shots for these little hot and readies.  

I most likely wont be back.  I think it's really stupid to charge cover for a bar that serves frozen drinks in plastic cups and plays music that sounds like it came from an ipod playlist :-/

text:
The service was actually excellent and we didn't experience any wait at weekend lunch hour. The waitress was helpful and very cheerful. The problem is.. lobster and crab were extremely salty. 

My friend and I started a discussion on why the lobster could end up being so salty. It seemed to my friend that the lobster was soaked in a salt bath and marinated. "Perhaps they probably stored the seafood in some brine for longer storage and forgot to wash the salt off", my friend postulated. I did't know if his guess was right, but we surely were defeat by the osmosis. I felt my mouth were getting dehydrated and shriveled up. We kept asking for water refill... After the meal, my friend and I both left with swollen sausage lips thanks to the salt. 

Revisit? I'll give it a miss.

I am one of those people who lives in the neighborhood, constantly lamenting the lack of cute places to shop for groceries.  Then, when such an establishment actually opens mere minutes from my home, i never go!

Shame on me.  I am trying to make up for my lack of patronage, though.  For example, i just stopped by pumpkin market after a hot yoga class.  I was craving a smoothie and pumpkin satisfied that craving - and then some!  I ordered the strawberry banana and it was darn tasty.  The cost, $4.75, raised my brow at first, but upon the first sip, i knew it was worth every penny.

While waiting for my smoothie, i perused the wares and pumpkin surely has what you need - produce, dairy, local meats, sweets, granola, pasta (i could go on).

Pumpkin market, keep delivering the goods and i promise not to ignore you ever again!

text:
We had dinner here on our last night in FL. We were trying to keep costs of our trip low and this dinner was our one splurge. 

The service was outstanding. The server was fantastic with recommendations and timing. We never felt rushed or hurried along through out our 2 hour meal. My guy and I like to savor and enjoy, ordering cocktails, wine, appetizers, entrees and sometimes dessert. This is the kind of place where you set the timing and we loved it! Our table was cleared of dishes and crumbs after each course. Our wine glasses were never empty nor were out water glasses. 

We both enjoyed the food immensely. Sometimes when we go out he orders better than me or vice versa, this time we ordered perfectly for our tastes. He was able to finish all of his, while I had leftovers to bring home. We both ate to until over full because it was delicious.

The atmosphere was nice. My only complaint was that there was a large conference going on at the resort so there were an abundance of people with name tags at every turn. Unfortunate timing on our part.

We felt it was worth the price point. It was a splurge but well worth it in our eyes. I would return here again the next time I am in town.

text: So happy I tried Pa & Ma's! First off, Ma was working the register and is CRAZY NICE! Explained the whole setup to me and the husband, every daily special, hours, the works. Super friendly and helpful. The food is served cafeteria style so walk up, select your main (we both got rib tips with sauce) and two sides. The portions are monstrous. Ridiculous for the money. Such a good deal! I got Mac'n'Cheese and beans with weanies. The Mac is fan-freaking-tactic. I'm usually 100% suspicious of restaurant Mac as it's always watery, gritty, and gross. This was hands down the best Mac ever. Creamy, legit cheese tasting, nice big shells/noodles. I could have just ate that and been content. Seriously get it! The baked beans were great. Perfect ratios of seasoning and sauce thickness. The hubby got mashed potatoes as a side and said they were definitely real potatoes. The rib tips were great. I absolutely love their BBQ sauce. I also ordered a dessert as I can't resist pie. It was a clear takeout container that I'm pretty sure was packed with three slices of apple pie. It was great! I wished I lived closer so I could come back more often! This is a winner folks! Prices are ridiculously affordable, you will have a full belly, you'll encounter super friendly staff, and you'll have terrific BBQ!

text:
This was an awesome Yelp event! I love the events where you can go during an open time frame and pick pretty much whatever you want.

I had only been to Banh Mi Boys once before when they first opened. I had been meaning to come back again, so this was the perfect opportunity!

I arrived near 5:30 in the evening. It was just the right time. They have about 6 parking spots in front and I was able to grab one. The "Greetings from Metairie" mural in front is great!! 

I was able to order right away. The staff was very friendly. I had hoped to order the Pork and Shrimp Spring Rolls, but the cashier informed me that all of the starters were sold out except for Crab Rangoons. So, I went with those. For my entree, I chose the Grilled Shrimp Banh Mi. As an extra purchase, I bought the Grilled Shrimp Vermicelli. They let me know I could wait in my car and they would bring my order out to me.

I did not have to wait very long. I'd say it was about 5-6 minutes and they were out with the order.

I brought it all home and my boyfriend and I split the items. All were great. We look forward to going back and trying some of the other options next time.

Thanks for the opportunity to try this place out again!!

text:
The pizza itself is pretty good. This is a chain pizza place from the North that opened up here recently. Hot oil is unique as well so I would recommend getting this if you eat spicy foods! It wasn't too spicy for me. The meatball was just okay. Not much flavor so I would give this topping a 3/5. Pepperoni and onion were also average. The crust is thin and crispy, which is how I like my pizza crust so if you like doughy crust, don't eat here. Pizza was ready pretty fast/on time. 

I couldn't give this 5 stars because the menu here is very limited. Only pizza, no sauces like hot sauce or ranch, and the toppings add up $$. A cheese pizza would be $10 but you add some toppings and suddenly it is a $20 pizza and the size isn't big. It's definitely not a "bang for your buck" type pizza joint. Hopefully this place gets to Uber eats or Doordash because right now, it's pickup only or dine in.

text:
I really appreciate these folks. My Maytag dryer, which we paid quite a bit for about seven years ago, was making a whining noise that was super annoying. What was worse, it was taking forever to dry the clothes. Not wanting to go and buy a new one I decided to have it repaired. I was prepared to have to wait weeks for an appointment but when I called they set something up for a few days out. They also advised that I go look at the area where the dryer vent exists the house to make sure there was no blockage. I like when a company tries to save me a little money by letting me know what I can do myself vs. having them do it. I had looked at that vent recently (or so I thought) so figured that was not an issue. The office was great about working around my schedule and when I gave them my availability for that day they scheduled me for in the morning with a two hour window and said the technician would call me that morning with an exact time.
Fast forward, he called, gave me a half hour window, and arrived as promised with his tools and a vacuum. The first thing we did was go outside and look at that vent, which it turns out was not working because the folks who recently painted out house had painted that too and it was stuck closed. Our technician, Salvador, got right to work and cleared that out as well as disassembled the dryer drum to see what was causing the whining noise. He spent a good ten or fifteen minutes vacuuming the insides of the dryer and quickly located the source of the noise.. Not only was it a relatively inexpensive repair, but he had the parts on his truck and was done very quickly.
Great service and highly recommend.

text:
I really do need to update this review!

This is very much our neighborhood go to place when needing some nourishment  when we don't feel like cooking, didn't make it to the grocery store, or have out of town guests.

We have been coming here for 10 years now and keep coming back for a reason - the service is good, the food is good, and they greet us like family.

This is not fancy cutting edge fusion food, but is basic Thai food. We almost always order the chicken saté as an appetizer. Their peanut sauce is the best anywhere. The pot stickers and spring rolls are also very good.

I very much enjoy their curry - my favorites are red , green  and panang. I also love their Bra Ram and Thai Cashew. My husband's favorite is drunken noodles. 

They have a full bar, Boba, and Thai tea. 

Service is quick and the food comes out fresh and perfectly cooked every time. They do takeout and delivery. 

So yes - I highly recommend Thai Corner.

text:
Last weekend, a friend of mine and I had just finished an amazing dinner at Bern's Steak House and were looking for somewhere to smoke a cigar. We had heard great things about Grand Cathedral Cigars, so headed over there for a nightcap and a stogie.

"Wow" was the first word out of my mouth when I first laid my eyes on the breathtaking gorgeous exterior of GCC. This remodeled church was recently converted into a cigar bar and it was quite extraordinary on the outside. 

The interior of Grand Cathedral Cigars was just as amazing. I was absolutely speechless at the complete transformation and decor of this place. The humidor was also very impressive. Arturo Fuente is one of my favorite cigar brands and GCC has a wide variety of them. 

Dominic was friendly and a pleasure to deal with when we chose our cigars. We were lucky to run into him later in the evening and he escorted us to the third level so we could explore more of the venue. 

We were there on a Saturday night so the bar was pretty packed. We thankfully didn't have to wait too long to receive our drinks. 

My only feedback is that there should be a couple more smoke eaters installed. There is also outside seating for those who are overwhelmed with the smoke. We were lucky enough to be on the third level where the smoke was not as prevalent. 

If you are a cigar smoker, you should definitely stop by Grand Cathedral Cigars. The ambiance, ample parking and the friendly staff make this place worth a visit. 

Thanks again to Dominic for the hospitality and we will be back soon!

text:
Boise Fry Company is connected to Press & Pony as they're owned by the same people. When I left Press & Pony I was told to leave out a side door rather than the front door or the elevator and I ended up right at the line to order here.

I ordered the BFC original ($6.95) and the purple fries ($3.65). The burger is a bit smaller than I expected and I guess that's fine as the price is cheaper than your typical burger, but regardless the star of the show here are the fries. They give them in silver bowls because they're fresh out of the fryer and tossed with some seasonings.

There are different types of potatoes/fries to choose from, from your typical russet and gold to interesting ones like purple. Because I wanted to go weird I aimed for ones I normally wouldn't be able to order at most other places and ordered the purple fries. They're different from your normal salty fries and are more earthy. There is a sauce station that has different sauces that you can pick and choose as your dipping accompaniment for your fries. If you can't choose which sauces to pair you can always try all of them.

It's a cool experience to try different potatoes in the state where the license plates read "Famous Potatoes".

text:
Small stores, lovingly run to share a particular culinary point of view, are my way to happiness. I collect them, like trading cards, often filling in my gap time with visits in order to get inspiration for my next cookery.

Ben's had me from the Stroopwafels, which I loved fresh in Amsterdam, and kept me with all the Dutch items, including more foodstuffs and even fan team scarves and shirts.

(I grew up in Soestdijk and Ben's brings back memories of both the foods and the way of shopping that's so different than the once-weekly big box shopping that's typical today.)

The deli counter is a big win, from traditional Italian salumi to smoked horse (tasty, salty!) to gouda to sub sandwiches (which, yes, at one time I called "grinders" and "hoagies").

Ben's: go there, enjoy the foodie love, take home some of the joy.

We are headed to a Wolf Pack football game this season and my wife and were both seeking some updated fan apparel. We started here because they usually have some great items that can be school bookstore specific. Overall though, their selection seemed lacking. I was used to a much larger range of products and instead felt there was a decent variety within only a few styles. A few sweatshirts, a few t-shirts, and few polos and shorts and then color/style options within that. They didn't have a large amount of fitted hats. I don't know if its due to the season, back to school, whatever the case but they also didn't have much inventory period. 
My wife found a cute cardigan for a reasonable price but that sadly, was the only purchase we made. 
Staff were nice but VERY new. When I asked for a fitting room they weren't sure how to get the key and sign off on the use sheet. Then we asked to put the cardigan on hold for a bit and they weren't sure how to hold something. They figured it out and the sweater was waiting at the counter when my wife returned to purchase it less than an hour later, but we didn't have high hopes initially. 
Also, they don't have a lot of small game time extras like face stickers, license plate frames, etc. I also wanted more alumni focused gear which I didn't find there but found at a competitor.

text:
This new location just opened up end of December in a strip mall located by the Costco across from the River Cree casino and resort.  They have a drive through as well as tables inside.  There's plenty of parking in the lot in front of the restaurant.

I came here right at dinner time, 6:00 expecting it to be super busy and it was.  Lined up right back to the front door!  There were two order takers and lots of staff cooking and packaging the orders so it went fairly quickly.  They were prepared for the rush since there were several full pans of cooked chicken ready to go and more constantly being brought out.

We didn't really know what to order so tried a few items.  The fried chicken was nice and crunchy and very fresh, not greasy at all.  The pieces look bigger than average and was juicy, not overcooked or dry.  The biscuit had just the right amount of salt and was moist and not crumbly.  The fries had Cajun seasoning but was barely spicy and delicious!  The tenders were bigger than other places and for five strips, you can choose two different dipping sauces.  We had the bold bbq and a hot one.  Nothing special with these sauces but the coating was crunchy and not greasy.

The chicken comes mild or spicy but they say the mild is like regular chicken and the spicy is NOT spicy.  They offer combos and platters, the difference being you get an extra side dish and a large drink instead of medium for the platter.  Looking at the menu, there are sandwich and seafood options like shrimp and fish.  The sides come in two sizes and the regular for $2.49 looks very small.

We really enjoyed the food here and will definitely go back to try more items.  Good location for people living in the west end!

text:
Dad calls me and says, "Have you been to Pizzeria Beddia? I read about it in Bon Appetit. Best pizza in America, Knowlton says." So I'm all like PFFFFFT, hell no. "Best Pizza??" Is there such a thing?! It's like if I'm in a room with Sofia Vergara, Charlize Theron and Halle Berry and you're like "Which one is the most beautiful??"  It's a DILEMMA people. I'd cuddle them all. And to further compound my problems, I went to their website where in ALL CAPS like a bad OKCupid Profile (why are you YELLING?) they instructed me that they make a limited number of pizzas and there's usually a line and please start lining up at 4pm for $30 pizzas. Not sure my boss would be cool with me leaving work at 3:30 to go stand in a line, guy. And you know I'm not gonna win a pizza on a weekend.

So, I called my between-jobs loves-food-as-much-as-me friend and was like, "I have this really really good idea.... You stand in line by yourself and bring fifty dollars in cash. For PIZZA." 

Thank God she agreed. Chomping on two pizzas later: Arribiatta pizza whose humorous description on the pizza board has the word "ANGRY"-- i.e. the poops you will have later on. But, oh so happily spicy. You're like crying and smiling at the same time. And then we had a sausage with onions-- I could eat a hundo slices. 

After much contemplating and processing I knew why this place was a special five star pizza (for me): It's *not* brick oven pizza. I know that sounds weird but I think 9/10 of the philly pizza places I regularly frequent are all brick oven places. Pizza that tastes this great and it was made in a gas oven-- that's a masterful skill. Like finding out Halle Berry is almost 50. Yeah. Gorgeous.

text: I wish I could give this 4 1/2 stars! A star is taken off because they only have one option for vegans, the vegan pancake. Also, I found out I have celiacs so I am gluten free and they cannot combine the vegan and gluten free pancake. They should make  options for vegans and gluten free people! Easy solutions would be having a bag of the vegan gluten free pancake mix. Making a Power bowl/Buddha bowl/healthy bowl- that would sell so quickly because now more than ever people are trying to eat healthy! Just throw in a bowl a bunch or sautéed veggies with brown rice or quinoa, with a slice of avocado. And there are tons of vegan gluten free egg mixes now as well as vegan gf cheeses (violife cheese tastes EXACTLY like the real thing) I'd be willing to pay $15 for vegan gluten free scrambled eggs with vegan gf cheese, even though it wouldn't take that much to make! (A whole bag of violife cheese is $4 at acme and "just egg" is only $4 - a suggestion serving size is 8) You could also keep vegan gf wraps on hand and you can make a breakfast burrito with the veggies, violife cheese and "just egg" and charge $18. It's frustrating to go to places and the only option is fruit. People for sure will appreciate you more with having the options and will pay more for things like these. Anyway thanks for listening! I hope one day you will incorporate one or two more things. We live right around the corner and would be there all the time!

text:
I absolutely loved yoga until I had a bad experience with an instructor. I took a 5 year break from yoga. Sparkle Yoga is bringing back the love. 

The instructors are super nice! They make an effort to remember your name and acknowledge you during class. The studio is clean and well stocked with supplies if you're a newbie without any yoga gear. 

Sparkle Yoga gives customers 20% off if one brings a receipt from Sprouts. There are no criteria when it comes to bringing a receipt from Sprouts for the discount. You don't need spend a certain amount at Sprouts nor do you need to have a receipt within a certain timeframe. I love local businesses that support other businesses. Plus 20% discounts make yoga much more reasonably priced. 

Sparkle Yoga is fantastic for the beginner as that's the only class I've taken. I assume they'd be great for experienced yogis. I highly recommend Sparkle Yoga!

I was running around today, trying to get some wood cut and the other HD's machine was down. Thankfully not here though. It took awhile to find a guy to help me so I was miffed. I also was considering renting tools but the price was so high I decided I would just buy a sander. So far it does the job but man oh man does holding a sander for hours feels like a workout.

It's got all the essentials here so I would come back if it wasn't the furthest Home Depot from my house. :)

text:
Definitely the best Indian I've had in the main line. For the future, I'll come here instead of Tiffin. 

We came in during prime restaurant time, Saturday night at 6:30, but they were able to find a table for us. Next time, however, I will call ahead because it was BUSY. 

We told our waiter we were vegan and he efficiently told us that we probably would want to order the roti (because as per usual naan wasn't dairy free). We also ordered the vegetable samosas to start, which were delightful including the yummy green sauce that accompanied them. The gobhi aloo dish, curried cauliflower and potatoes were delicious. The baingan bharta dish, roasted eggplant, was decadent and delicious. 

I will definitely return and this might become one of my favorite take-out spots too.

biz_attributes_clean: GoodForKids: True, RestaurantsTakeOut: True, RestaurantsTableService: False, HappyHour: False, BikeParking: True, BusinessAcceptsCreditCards: True, BusinessAcceptsBitcoin: False, Caters: True, RestaurantsReservations: False, WheelchairAccessible: True, RestaurantsGoodForGroups: True, RestaurantsPriceRange2: 2, RestaurantsAttire: u'casual', HasTV: False, DogsAllowed: False, Ambience: {'touristy': False, 'hipster': None, 'romantic': False, 'divey': False, 'intimate': False, 'trendy': None, 'upscale': False, 'classy': False, 'casual': True}, Alcohol: u'none', WiFi: u'no', RestaurantsDelivery: True, GoodForMeal: {'dessert': False, 'latenight': False, 'lunch': True, 'dinner': None, 'brunch': False, 'breakfast': False}, BYOB: True, ByAppointmentOnly: False, OutdoorSeating: True, BusinessParking: {'garage': False, 'street': True, 'validated': False, 'lot': False, 'valet': False}, NoiseLevel: u'average'
text:
Baology first had me at bao! But when it added in tantalizing subject matter like Taiwanese fried chicken and all those dumplings it quickly became my kind of science!

At its core Baology is modern Taiwanese food with a splash of other Asian influences served simple. Depending on what time you come, the tablet POS system up front will showcase a menu for breakfast, lunch or dinner, And depending on when you come in you might find noodle dishes, bento box style bian dangs, incredible ruen bing wraps, or a good sized helping of 3 cup chicken. But for whatever occasion you come in, you might want to wash your food down with their cold brewed hibiscus and ginger tea.

For my first visit I was craving those baos, and ordered in a trio. Making the mistake of thinking it wasn't enough food I also ordered some shrimp & pork dumplings which were a sweet and savory explosion! With 5 baos on the menu it was tricky to choose at first, but I enjoyed the following:

King oyster mushroom bao - the vegetarian bao on the menu and its incredible with its pickled mustard greens, crispy garlic, and hoisin sauce that blends impressively with the mushroom king.

Roasted Berkshire pork belly bao - the fatty and tender pork belly gets accentuated by togarashi aioli, pickled daikon radish, cilantro, and crushed peanut powder!

Taiwanese fried chicken bao - the perfect preview to their fried chicken reigns supreme with lemon aioli and Thai basil!

After enjoying the bao I had to try more of that fried chicken, and it proved to be crispy saucy perfection with just a bit of spice. With two good visits under my belt its enough to have me craving more!

text:
Nothing but great things to say. This was our first visit and won't be our last! Parking is interesting if you've never been before. The lots connected to the restaurant itself are tiny, however we noticed some folks parking across the street.

Once inside, the place is nice! Great hostess stand up front, plenty of waiting area, seating inside, upstairs, and out on the patio. TVs everywhere! Great place to watch a game or hang out and drink at the bar. Decent selection of local drafts.

We had an excellent, friendly, and super capable server who never wrote down a thing all night. Remembered everything we wanted and checked on us regularly even tho is was a busy weekend night. 

Ultimately it's about the food tho. The hot chicken poutine is excellent (and could be an entree for someone all on it's own). Everyone in our party got something different so we could sample a variety of items. Everything was delicious! Super excited to go again soon. Something caught my eye on the menu and if we hadn't have really wanted to get the poutine, we'd gotten the Hot Chicken Chorizo Queso. Man, does that sound good!

The place ain't cheap, expect to spend about $15-20/pp, but you'll get great service, delicious food, and have a fun time!!

text: Honestly, this is one of my favourite places in the city. It's my local pub and I always have a good time here, hands down. The staff are lovely and treat their regulars like gold, service is usually fantastic, even when they're really busy, and who doesn't like their dangles? The Brewhouse has no pretensions--they're a pub/bar and they serve great pub/bar food, along with having a number of excellent specials on throughout the week (my favourites are Wing Wednesday and Shrimp Thursday). Definitely check it out to watch a hockey game or just to grab drinks and snacks with friends. And don't forget to try a Ninja Turtle Dangle!

text:
This place is good. I've heard about it for awhile and a couple of friends and I decided to check it out for brunch one Sunday. 

Service was good. They do bottomless mimosas which is the best part. I had the shrimp & grits for my meal. The only thing I would request next time is more sauce. The sauce wasn't enough for the grits. The grits were delicious cheese grits. Nice consistency and enough cheese and butter. The shrimp were cooked perfectly, just needed a bit more of that sauce. The sauce was so good too. I also didn't care for the potato hash...it's not a hash...it's hash browns and it was so dry... I didn't enjoy that at all. However, I most definitely will be back. There were a couple of other items I wanted to try out. 

Keep up the good work.

text:
I had a very positive experience at Viero Bridal. It started off a little rocky because Anastasia had to push my appointment back 20 minutes, but we made up for that time and it wasn't technically her fault (the prior appointment was running late). This was my 3rd bridal shop appointment so I wasn't interested in going into all the details about my wedding, and I didn't want to have a long conversation about the pros and cons of every single dress I tried on. Anastasia was very efficient, courteous, and she gave us space to talk privately (literally and figuratively). She was quick to pull dresses and help me try them on. I must have tried on at least 10 dresses and she was a good sport throughout the whole process. The only things I would change is brighter lighting and providing a small stool to stand on; I'm short so I looked like I was swimming in the dresses. 

I did some research on the two brands they offer (Viero Bridal and Milla Nova) prior to visiting so I knew I wanted. Their Viero designer is based in Chicago so she can make adjustments to the Viero dresses when you order. Also the prices are VERY fair for what they are! I would recommend going to this bridal shop after you have a sense of what you want, and checking out their website.

categories: Yelp Events, Local Flavor
biz_attributes_clean: None
text:
Thank you Yelp Philly and honeygrow for this experience! 

What a great weekday treat! 

We've ordered from honeygrow before and couldn't give up a chance to not go back! The drive over to this new location was about 20 minutes or so - not too bad during a workday. The location is in a large plaza (so lots of parking) and for this particular day, they had a large sign advertising their grand opening the following day - as a Yelp experience, we were given the privilege to come one day prior to opening!

For crowd control reasons, we were delegated time slots to enter. Once inside it's a good size, with the full ordering bar on the left walking in and the sit down dining area to the right. The ordering process was very simple, using the kiosk to place the order (payment can be collected this way too) and then while I waited, I grabbed some fountain drinks - a strawberry/guava lemonade and a açaí/pomegranate drink - both delicious! 

The food was readied in about 15 minutes and I had asked to take it to go, so they did a great job of packing everything up neatly and all the necessary items were provided to enjoy the meal. 

The meals were hearty, well portioned, full of flavor, and hit all the hunger pains for lunch time!

The workers were so great and diligent, from the check in process, food prep, kiosk questions - thank you for all the help and patience!! 

Great to know this place exists and looking forward to more visits!

text:
This is a pretty good market, but I'm writing a review about a certain cooked food dish. 
I'm talking about some of the best Jerk Chicken in the area.  I find myself craving for it constantly. I usually purchase it once a week or so. Their chicken is perfectly spiced and grilled on an open flame, resulting in a juicy, charred to perfection bird. 
I usually get the whole chicken, which is butterflyed into a flat piece of meat. This enables the flame to reach every part of the bird.  Juicy and moist, with a crispy skin, engrossed with the powerful Jerk seasoning and just the right amount of salt.  Good eating! I accompany my lovely bird with a pint container of baked mac & cheese and a pint of sweet potato puree.  Both sides are delicious but no matter what you do don't order the collard greens!  They are hideous. The whole bird and two sides is less than 10 bucks! A real savings. 

News flash: I've tried this dish at other ShopRite markets and it wasn't nearly as good. So if you have to drive a few extra miles, do it, it will be well worth the trip.

text:
You've tried the rest, now try the best,

This course has something for everyone.  And if you do all the obstacle courses you will use up the 2.5 hours as advertised, but depending on your level of comfort with height and how much challenge you want to take on your milage may vary.  It's nice that they have different price points for kids as some of the courses are extremely challenging even for adults.  Granted this adult has almost no upper body strength so take it for what it's worth.  So the beginner course is level 1, level 2a and 2b are both good intermediate level of challenge, level 3 is all zip line with 1 or 2 obstacle courses.  level 4 is OK until you get to the choice of doing the "extension" or "exit".  If you want the full challenge, go for the extension, but you have been warned.  It is extremely difficult so save it for last if you want to get the other obstacles done first.  The one thing i don't like about the course is htat you have no option to pass someone if there's someone slower than you in front. some of the other courses have bypasses at the connection points between obstacles so you can pass someone along the way.  Other than that, a great fun outting for kids and adults!!

text:
Adobe is the casual Mexican restaurant South Philly needs, but perhaps not the one it deserves - it's a bit better than that.

Adobe has a menu with scads of options (including great vegetarian and vegan ones), from burrito to tacos to quesadillas to enchiladas, plus reasonably priced pitchers of margaritas or sangria, fair beer options, lots of seating, a bar with a solid layout (bar is in the center of the room, with the physical bar encircling), and to boot, a solid karaoke night (that is, free, and an interesting yet strange mixture of attendees).

Adobe keeps me coming back with all of these things combined. Do I wish the prices were a little lower? Sure. But when you see that $12-15 entree, remember that it's probably going to come platter style, with scads of food available (also see intro: this is a casual Mexican restaurant - if you expect something else, this is not your place). I often get a quesadilla with vegan cheese (~$9) which is easily enough for me compared to an entree.

text:
The first rule of Sushi Club?  That no one talks about how many times I sent those wipe off menus back with more rolls and apps aaaaaand...yeah.  We won't talk about that. 

Having visited the Westside location, I was PUMPED to learn that they were adding a location up north!  

Truth be told, Sushi Club totally caters to those who prefer 'non-traditional' rolls full of cream cheese and tempura and deep fried goodness and a TON of things all in one roll.  If you are a sushi purist, you may wanna steer clear and hit a place like Sakura (our fav, honestly).  However, for less than $25 at Sushi Club you can eat to your heart's content - that's right - all the miso soup, salads, crab rangoon, gyoza, rolls, and even dessert you want - all for one price (changes depending on the day of the week and if it is a holiday).  PRO TIP: Pay close attention to how many pieces come in each roll - some have 3 and some have 8 - and you are charged if you leave more than 7 pieces on the table ($1 per piece) - so don't let your eyes get bigger than your stomach!  

Long story short - Sushi Club is PERFECT for that girl's night or date night when you just wanna sit and chat and keep ordering whatever the table thinks sounds good without worrying about how much it will cost or splitting the bill evenly. Service was good.  Is it my favorite in town?  Naw.  But for the value and sheer gluttony of it - 5 stars.

text:
This review is primarily based on the customer service, or lack thereof, I received this morning. To provide some context, Krispy Kreme is running a promotion that allows you to purchase a dozen glazed donuts for $5. This is half off the usual price. Now, I think their donuts taste like air, but my son likes them, so I thought it would be a nice surprise.

I dropped my daughter off at school and checked the app. The app read that the hot now light was on, so I headed on over to the shop. I pulled into the drive thru to place my order. 

Me: I'd like a dozen glazed donuts.
Leslie: That'll be $10.92.
Me: They're not $5?
Leslie: Do you have a coupon?
Me: A coupon for what?
Leslie: A coupon to get them for $5?
Me: I thought they were $5 when the Hot Now light is on.
Leslie: 2nd window
Leslie: $5.46
Me: I hand her my money.
Leslie: Provides my change.
Leslie: Hands me the donuts.
Me: Thank you.
Leslie: Nothing

What kind of customer service requires me to tell you that I know about your promotion and, therefore, you should honor it. Firstly, if the promotion is a dozen donuts for $5 when the hot now light is on, I should be able to place my order and get them for the promotional price. I shouldn't have to tell you I know about the promotion. 

Secondly, a coupon is not required to take advantage of the promotion, so why did she ask me for one?

Thirdly, she didn't say "ok, you can pull up or drive to the second window." Oh, no, she said "second window." She didn't even give me a new total. And, of course, she put the icing on the cake by not acknowledging my thank you. 

No, this experience was not worth it. To get me hot and bothered this early in the morning is not right. Deleting the Krispy Kreme app now. Get your $5/dozen glazed donuts on Fridays from Buttermilk Drop on St. Bernard.
