New looking place. Nice big garage door windows so you can see where they brew their beer. This place is way bigger than I thought was when I first walked in. Lots of indoor and outdoor seating.  The indoor seating is a bit more formal feeling while the outdoor seating is more first come first serve. 
   The beer here is pretty good. They did have a good selection of their own beer along with some from local breweries like cigar city and bus brew (which might be the same as Florida ave...). Had a chance to try their sours, some IPA's and a stout. It was a good time. 
   Their food is not on par for what the price is. I tried there Bibimbap, which for anyone who has had actual bibimbap, this one feels Americanized. They did do the rice pretty well, had that dries crunchy rice feel you get from the bottom of the pot. The "kimchi" was just slaw and the bulgogi was just ground beer with sauce. Anyways, also tried the cheesesteak eggs rolls, those were pretty good. But you get two roll for $9. :(

text: I was lucky enough to being invited to a Dinner Lab event recently and I was so excited to be a part of the experience. The event was held in the Junior League building Uptown and had a pleasant setup with long tables and folding chairs. We were greeted at the door by a person checking names and the bar offering drinks to the guests. We were then shown to our seats at one of the tables. The staff was very friendly and set up seats so that my friend and I could sit together. Our chef for the night was from August and was doing a take on breakfast for dinner. The dinner and drink pairings were amazing. There were six courses of small plates and two drink pairings. The service was excellent and I never had an empty glass in front of me for long. The company during dinner...ehhh...yeah. My friend and I had some delightful conversations that were not really helped by our dining companions. I suppose the business can't be blamed by that though. The event itself was very pleasant and ran smoothly. I would go again if someone invited me, but I don't think I would get a membership. The food was impressive, but I would rather spend the money on a nice meal out with my friends.

text:
Bulldog Yoga is a well-kept secret that I'm almost too possessive to share! 

The instructors are very warm, welcoming, and really know their stuff. All three intensity levels are a great workout and offer lots of opportunities for modifications to step either up or down, depending on how your body is feeling. Although all classes follow the same general flow, no two are ever exactly alike, which keeps it both challenging and fun. I also love the physical adjustments provided by some of the instructors whether it's to correct form, to deepen a stretch, or to help open up. 

NOTE: you will definitely sweat, especially in the 92-degree classes!

The studio itself is clean, bright, and very energizing. I love that there are mirrors scattered around the walls so you can check your form from more than one angle. It is also good to know that mats are provided so if you don't have one, or forget to bring yours, it's no problem. Hot tea is offered in the back.

All in all, I have had a great experience at this studio and I am really pleased with my practice. In a few short weeks, I have noticed myself getting stronger and it's a great feeling! I highly recommend checking it out if you're a beginner or expert yogi--the first class is free, what do you have to lose?

text:
First time visit to Simon's as heard great things of local restaurant.  Party of 5 sat on outdoor patio which was covered and nice during a hot afternoon.  I ordered half sandwich ( roast beef) & French Onion soup for $8.85.  Sandwich bottom was served wet so was mush to eat and could not put back down on plate as it would fall apart.  Soup was good, albeit quite salty but a good amount of cheese topping.  All in all , service was mediocre but for price I was happy as we're friends. Shock and 3- star rating comes due to fact that my bill arrived for $11.85 as my friends all had a $3 price increase.  We inquired into why and waitress told us 'see that sign'... sign was a typed 8x11 small font sheet placed 6' away from front door out of sight for anyone entering premise stating a $3 surcharge on all menu items!  
What?! Can Management not place stickers on menu stating the price increase not reflected on menu and/or have waitress/staff inform customers before ordering?  This is almost a 30% increase! Hate to cheat the staff, but it was hard to give them a healthy tip as waitress could've pointed this out to us. I get with Covid business may be slow, but here we are trying to support local and you are kind of raping us in your sneaky ways and a $3 surcharge? Highway robbery at its finest.  So I'm thinking 'surcharge' will you drop down prices later?  Doubtful.  
Management, please put stickers on your menus till new ones are printed you cheapskate!

text:
I have a coworker who is always suggesting we eat at places no one in the office has heard of.  This time it was Gramercy Park Pizza and Grill.

My first impression is that it's a small place and I don't particularly care for small places.  I like being able to move around and not knock into tables and chairs.  The first employee we interacted with was very friendly.

Ordering was easy.  We had a group of 7.  6 of the 7 orders came out correct but the 7th order was completely off.  There is a burger with all the fixin's including hash browns and sausage gravy.  The 7th order was said burger sans hash browns and gravy.  When the order was delivered to the table, the burger was COVERED in unwanted gravy.  The server was alerted to the issue and with a "sorry bro", the burger was whisked away for a new one.

I ordered the El Capitan which is a chicken sandwich on chibatta bread with Franks Red Hot sauce, blue cheese crumbles, coleslaw and jalapeños.  I hate to say it but it was just alright.  It was small and not filling at all.  I figure if I'm paying $10+ for a sandwich, I had better be filled up when I leave.

For me, I won't be back.  There was nothing about my experience that blew my mind besides being hungry 2 hours later.  I suppose if I would have spent $20 or more on my meal, I would have been full but I wasn't willing to pay that much.

P.S. - There is a full bar and I believe it was weekdays when they have a happy hour from 3pm to 6pm.  We were there around noon so I have no idea what the specials are.  Well, I think I remember being confused by a can of $2 Rainier Beer or something else from the Pacific Northwest that no one should pay $2 a can for.

text:
Don't let this listing fool you because although S&B is a catering company and event venue that specializes in weddings, social events, and corporate parties, they also offer food pick up! My husband and I recently placed an order for some BBQ and it was really delicious.

We found this business on Yelp Philly's Black-Owned Businesses Collection and I am so happy we decided to give them a try. They are currently offering BBQ style take out on Saturdays which is perfect since we have jumped right into summer. We ordered two platters: BBQ chicken and BBQ pulled pork, and each of them came with two sides. For the first platter, he decided on Mac and cheese and potato salad and I chose coleslaw and Mac and cheese for the second platter. 

The food was delicious. The pulled pork was really flavorful and I loved the smoky taste it had to it. I'm a big fan of coleslaw and have tried tons of different kinds so I'm happy to say that I fully approve of theirs. The finely shredded cabbage tasted fresh and had a nice crunch to it, and the dressing wasn't overly creamy. The Mac and cheese was simple but good. I tried a bite of the chicken my husband got and it was also great but I am definitely in favor of the pulled pork! Sodiah, who is the owner, told me they're known for their ribs so maybe we'll try that next time!

In addition to the tasty food, I was impressed with the streamlined ordering process. The entire ordering and pick up process was done through Sodiah who was professional, helpful, and friendly. After I called to place my order, she sent me a text message confirming my food selections, the price, and the pick up date and time. She also sent me thorough instructions explaining that due to Covid-19, I should call when I get there, remain in my vehicle, and they would bring the food out to me. I was given the option to pay with cash, credit card, CashApp, or Venmo. I chose Venmo and this further confirmed that there was absolutely no contact! 

I'm so excited to have discovered this place and look forward to future pick ups. Follow the business on Instagram for updated information and the weekly menu - @snbeventconcepts. :)

text: Phenomenal in every way, from the comfort of the chairs to the service and the food. 5-stars all around. We went on a Saturday afternoon to try this new DTSP restaurant and was not disappointed. We had reservations but arrived early (grand prix). We were sat at a table immediately in a mostly empty restaurant. The server, Chloe, was on point, friendly and attentive. We mentioned our allergies at the table and it was no problem. She seemed to know her menu well and made some suggestions. We ordered chicken & waffles, short rib, pot pie and chicken parm for dinner and baby kale Caesar & PBJ wings to start. Wow. Talk about flavor explosion! I was skeptical about the PB&J wings but ended up delightfully surprised. They were Thai inspired and made perfect sense once that was clear. The baby kale Caesar was my least fav as it had a heavy anchovy overtone all the way thru. I love Caesar salad and this was fresh and tasty minus the fishy fishy taste- not enough of an issue to dock stars tho. Great presentation and the toasty points served with it were super yummy. My meal was the chicken and waffles with bacon butter & maple syrup. This is not a normal item I order but it spoke to me. Two very hearty pieces of chicken were placed atop a beautiful Round waffle. I used a fork to cut into the chicken and to my surprise it just melted apart- no knife needed. The flavors were incredible. I ate the whole dang thing, it was so good. Everyone at the table enjoyed their meal immensely. Go there. Eat. Enjoy all the ways this place makes you happy. They have coke zero, extra stars for this. All in all a great time.

text:
I have lived in 4 states now and experienced my share of DMV offices. Like others, I agree that the new location is not centrally located for the Reno-Sparks area. It's about 20 minutes for me from NW Reno.

Pluses include the new clean building with lots of light, clean bathrooms (including a family bathroom that I used to nurse my baby while waiting) and friendly staff. Because it took my teen three tries to pass the knowledge test, we were at the DMV three times in three weeks. Parking was always a breeze, probably due to the large lot and required appointments.

All the staff we encountered were friendly and supportive with the teen. With the first appointment, we were in and out in 30 minutes. They let you re-test within 7 days as a walk-in. I was a little nervous about not having an appointment the second time, but again we were in and out in no time. However, the third time, there were a lot of people in the appointment line, and they were taken care of first. Once a third staff member came to the area, things went faster for us in the walk-in line.

Once my teen passed the test (yay) we had to wait a while to get the permit and picture taken but that's because there were a bunch of teens that passed that afternoon so they were all waiting too.

Overall, I think this DMV did a fine job for tasks that no one finds very pleasant.

text:
If you need flooring, particularly any type of tile, this is the place to go!  They have soooo much to choose from.  I think almost anyone can find something that's perfect for their home and personal taste.  We actually came here for glass tile for a backsplash, and they had exactly what I was looking for.  And their prices were excellent.  Much better than every other place we've priced out, and our contractor said their material is actually high quality, too.  So I was pretty pleased with that.

The store is huge, well organized, well stocked, and very clean.  There are usually some employees walking around, but it seems like they're always busy with other customers.  So you'll have to get in line, or go to the front for more help.  I think the employee friendliness/helpfulness has been very hit or miss, too.  One guy was flat out rude at check out while buying a small amount of samples.  Another girl was very helpful in explaining their stocking situation.  I guess it just depends on the time of day.

Overall, I like this store, and I'm going back this weekend to buy more tile.

text:
Seriously cannot recommend this office enough. I've been a glasses wearer since 7th grade.. a long time ago now... and have had a slew of issues finding a good eye doctor. Everything from unorganized/unprofessional offices, lost appointments and contacts, to weird tacked on fees and hugely over priced visits.
It had gotten to the point where I just avoided going all together. Before a recent visit to South Tampa Eyecare it has probably been a good 4 or 5 years, thankfully my glasses have served me well.
But I had been a little tired of the same thing, wanted to get contacts and just make sure everything was okay, so I did a google search and settled on this office.
So glad I did.
Making an appointment was easy, with only a couple days wait. The office is comfortable and just had a nice atmosphere in general. Everyone who works here is incredibly friendly and helpful, and Dr. Helsing is great, thorough, and kind.
It's such a relief to have finally found an eye doctor I'm happy to return to.

text:
We were STARVING when we arrived to New Orleans after a late flight and after some quick surveying of the late night food delivery landscape, this place seemed to be our best bet. 

I'm vegetarian and unfortunately the vegetarian options at this place are scarce. They have breakfast, sandwiches with every single kind of ingredient you could imagine, specialty sandwiches, and sides. 

Both GF and I elected to order the fries po'boy bc why the fuck would you not? I meant to order both with all the fix ins (tomato, lettuce, and a condiment) but the "fix ins" appeared in our cart as a separate order which I stupidly deleted. 

We also ordered regular fries, and cheese fries (as if we briefly forgot we ordered a po'boy with fries) and mac and cheese. 

The food arrived within expected time but unfortunately did not contain the mac and cheese. After running through the suspects that could have been responsible for this blasphemy, I noticed an email with the refund for the missing item. It turns out they were out of mac and cheese at 1am. 

So, in case you were wondering, the fries po'boy without the fix ins is literally bread and fries. It paired nicely with the rest of our order (cheese fries and regular fries). Fortunately, the fries here are pretty good. They're thicker than I usually prefer but very crispy. The thickness provides for a nice, meaty, fry. This meal was as enjoyable as bread and fries could be. 

I'd be curious to try the sandwich again with the fix ins.

text:
There comes a point in life when something clicks and you suddenly realize you are in public....Holding 9 shots of Jameson, 4 cans of PBR and a minty coffee beverage that tastes like the soul of an innocent girl scout.

Surrounded by graffiti.
Its dark.
Its dirty.
And its sticky. 

You look around.

What day is it?
What was I doing here? 
Where did I come from?

Was I just roaming around in a frenzy of some kind? 
Who are these people, these faces? 
Where do they come from? 

You're right in the middle of a fucking reptile zoo, and somebody is giving booze to these goddamn things. 

It won't be long now before they tear you to shreds.

Panicked, you make it through the double doors at the front entrance and realize you are 1 block off Magazine street.

The sun is out.

It's only Tuesday.

And you're at the Saint.

text: It was a special occasion with a couple of birthdays, so several of us went to Charley's.  We had reservations & were taken quickly into a very comfortable room.  The lights were low & the aura was relaxing.  I ordered bronzed salmon to be cooked well done, with a side of fried green tomatoes.  The food was excellent, but each item had a slight flaw.  This resulted in my giving Charley's 4 stars versus 5.  The flaw with the salmon is that I requested it well done, but the salmon I received was not well done.  The outside of it looked well done, but the inside of the salmon had a very raw region through the interior.  That was disappointing.  The fried green tomatoes were excellent, but the tomatoes were very thick & extremely hard.  I personally would have preferred tomatoes a little thinner & certainly not so tough to sink my teeth into.  The flavors were awesome though..  it was a fun 4-star evening.

text:
Antioch has the pleasure of being a school with an exceptional "cafeteria" on its campus. Because Book Ends more of a deli/cafe and the food is sourced super fresh and locally - often just a few feet away at the Saturday farmers market. But the cafe is on the top floor of the campus building and anyone is welcome to dine there. 

It's a hidden gem, because it can be hard to find if you're not an Antioch student. You can reach the cafe by going through the building, or taking the outside steps straight to the top floor, which are located near the Cota entrance of the city lot where the Saturday market takes place. So it's a great place to grab a bite after market. Or during class hours. 

Outdoor seating? OH YES, and you know how much everyone loves eating outdoors. The cafe has some seats inside, but on the rooftop there are loads of tables and lounging sofas, including long dining tables, 4 tops and some intimate 2 tops which are shared with the Antioch people. 

The cafe has a selective menu of half a dozen or so sandwiches, and half a dozen or so salads. All super fresh and tasty. There's an option for a half sandwich and half salad/soup for $9-10. 
It's appropriate to say the offerings here are "well-curated" because it's a small space and everything that's offered strives to be local, whether it's the ingredients in the food they prepare on site, or items they source from other businesses. Such as: Renaud's croissants, McConnell's ice cream, or Caribbean Coffee on tap. 

The atmosphere is casual, and food comes out on cute paper plates and metal trays, reminding me of cafeteria-style eating. But not cafeteria-style food.

It's a great spot to escape above the bustle of downtown. I'm partial to the brisket banh mi and the Israeli couscous salad, but everything here is delicious. Yep, they even have the ever-popular kale salad here too.

text:
I signed up for online ordering today, expecting to be able to pick it up at the drive thru (I did not want to go in to the store, because they barely (if at all) enforce mask policies or social distancing... even for their employees. So I placed an online order... went to get it at the drive thru, and was told I could only pick it up inside (so why even bother having online ordering?) As I walked in there was an employee wearing a very loose mask, under her nose.  I have seen the same employee do the same thing every time I have been here, and she is in front of the counter... interacting with customers, seating them. I got my order and left. 

According to my doctor,  I am high risk (as in I will probably die) if I catch Covid. I actually do not eat out... and seldom even do takeout except for a few places I trust.

PDQ is no longer on my list of safe places.

Food? It was PDQ! Decent food, priced a little high. Not anything worth taking a risk for. I did file a complaint with the PDQ Customer Service... maybe I will update this after (if) I hear from them.

Florida is currently #1 in the nation for the new UK variant of COVID... which is much more contagious than any of the other strains. So please, wear a mask and social distance! If not for yourself, and your family... than do it for those of us who are older and high risk.  Forget the stupid politics... be a good human!   https://www.washingtonpost.com/health/ukvariant-coronavirus-us-spread/2021/02/07/a197dbc2-680a-11eb-8468-21bc48f07fe5_story.html?

text:
If you try to describe Donkey's Place to people it can be a hard sell.  They are in Camden, NJ, not Philadelphia and making cheesesteaks on a poppy seed kaiser roll.  Now that is out of the way, Donkey's Place is worth it.  I have been on this weird cheesesteak kick and Donkey's Place did not disappoint.  I ordered my usual with whiz and onions and the second I ate it, I knew I would be thinking about it for months after.  Seriously, it is one of my favorite cheesesteak places.  It's small and has about three parking spots, which are hard to get, even on non busy times, but there is street parking.  
Honestly, if I'm in Camden or even close to Camden, I'll be coming to get their cheesesteaks.

biz_attributes_clean: BikeParking: True, RestaurantsTakeOut: True, BusinessAcceptsCreditCards: True, BusinessParking: {'garage': False, 'street': False, 'validated': False, 'lot': True, 'valet': False}, RestaurantsPriceRange2: 3, Caters: True
text:
We don't do our weekly shopping here. This place is like a fine restaurant only reserved for special occasions. Of course that's because of me and my spendthriftiness. I once left here with a $215 bill and my cart wasn't even full. So why then does this place still get 5 stars in my book?

The food is so damn good. The selection is so damn big. They have an entire coffee bean section. Which is funny because if you've seen the joint it's not that large. Comparatively speaking. 

We've hosted a few in home parties and gotten their sandwich trays. These are so excellent. Like who raves about a sandwich tray?!? Order one for your next gathering. You'll get it. 

I can't leave without grabbing some premade sushi. Just can't. 

THE CHEESE SECTION. 

We got a ham for Christmas and didn't have to do anything but heat it up. It was amazing quality. 

Yeah I just really like this place. My wife gets so excited to shop here. 

Staff is super friendly and helpful. They take you to an item. They don't point. They also don't hide.

I felt tired. Tired of projects and assignments, tired of e-mail notes and text messages, tired of work to be done around the house and yard. I decided to give up my wintertime barber, "Wahlithium" (that's the Wahl Lithium Ion Pro Series Clipper), and try something new.

At SC, I was offered the Double MVP, and took it. It's a haircut, hot towel treatment, massaging shampoo, and neck and shoulder massage, and more hot towel, invigorating shampoo, and neck and shoulder massage.  It being my first visit, I couldn't really say whether it's "double," but it was nice. I watched sports, and forgot about all the projects-assignments-e-mails-text-messages. In the end, my hair was cut the way I wanted it, and I wasn't tired anymore. Thanks, SC.

text:
My Dining Trio (Husband Gary, mom and I) realized that Stray Dogs just opened recently, so we of course had to try it. And it was a very positive experience! The former owner of Zona 78 transformed his River Road-based restaurant (just east of Oracle) into this light and delightful eatery. It favors those coming for pizza/burger/fries/salad/sandwiches, along with a good beer or drink. But we loved it even without a drink. It works for families, friends and anyone wanting to hang and enjoy good eats.

The food was all good quality, and prepared with pride and finesse. I was pleasantly surprised at the reasonable prices, considering the good quality prep and ingredients. Mom's salmon was a healthy 6 oz., served with coleslaw on a plank. Gary and I enjoyed the Island Burger (Hawaiian style) and the Shroomburger, served in casual cardboard boxes on a soft, tasty brioche bun. I donated my bread to mom, as I don't do much gluten. She made a great sandwich out of the salmon, although it was tasty just as it was. We also ordered handmade fries with garlic and Parmesan (real Parmesan flakes). YUM. Gary practically did them in himself, before remembering that we were sharing. That speaks highly of them. 

NOTE: they offer a gluten-free pizza crust at an additional cost. I didn't get to try it on this visit. But, I'll be baaack!

The service was friendly and the food came quite quickly. I understand the restaurant just recently opened (just over a week ago). Everything was seamless. We enjoyed the crisp, light and friendly ambiance, and noted the outdoor casual dining area (with umbrellas) for the future. And true to the name of the establishment, they really do have a fenced area for dogs, complete with fire hydrant! Didn't see any stray dogs hanging out in it while dining there today, LOL.

Let your buddies know about them. You'll enjoy their food and so will your wallet!

text:
Located on Whyte avenue, this has always been a birthday favourite.  Whenever we are dining on Whyte, Block 1912 is the coffee and dessert destination. 

I absolutely adore the homey feel of this place.  All the furniture is mismatched, couches and big comfy chairs in some of the seating areas.  So much charm and warmth still.

We go here for the gelato and the desserts.  Both have always been awesome!  

I am not a huge fan of their coffee, but I do like the selection of teas and tea lattes.  

The desserts are not just cakes, there is everything from Grandma's confetti squares to panna cotta and raspberry creme brulee.  Anything I have had the opportunity to try has been awesome!  

Rather than having to indulge in a whole cake for a birthday, Block 1912 is a great option, even for a group!  Everyone gets their own little slice of heaven! 

Parking on Whyte is always a nightmare, but there is a pay lot located just south of 1912.

text:
I came here for lunch with my coworkers. The choice of places to go were Patachou (a regular staple of mine), Shoefly Public House (never been and I recommended), and Ralston's. Several people in the group had been to Ralston's before and it just seemed to be the most convenient choice. 

It was my first time at Ralston's and at first impression is a regular sports bar with a shuffle board and a couple of dart boards. I would never have guessed that they would have gastro pub food here.

I got the Peachy Pig flatbread with rosemary potatoes. I like that there was a variety of sides that you could choose from. To go with your dish. The place also has a large drink menu. I thought my dish was awesome, the pulled pork and peach chipotle were perfect together and they also gave a good amount of meat. The Rosemary potatoes were okay, I felt that they were a bit over cooked, having the slightly getting to the burnt point flavor and a bit hard. 

Everyone else also seemed to really like their food and our server was really nice. That's why I'm giving Ralston's 4 stars, although I felt it bordered 3-3.5 starts.

text:
PSA: Don't bother agreeing to a late reservation.

Our party of 4 showed up for an 8:45 reservation and were told it would be 5-10 minutes, as a table of 4 was just finishing up. Because there is no free space to stand in the restaurant, and because it was approximately 110 degrees inside, we opted to wait outside. Ten minutes later, we came back in and were told the bill had been paid so it should just be another minute. We peeked our heads inside here and there, and the table of 4 continued drinking wine, looking at their phones, talking, etc. No sign of them leaving. 

We figured we would give it another 10 minutes. Still nothing. I would think the restaurant would have asked the group to vacate because a reservation was behind them, but apparently Zeppoli is swimming in profits and did not need our business. At 9:15, we were hungry, it was late, and this is a restaurant that closes at 10pm, so we asked them to cancel the reservation so we could move on. An apology was offered, and a promise made that if we called back on any day we would get seated right away, but if we could not be seated with a reservation we'd had for a month, where did they plan on seating us at a moment's notice?

I was looking forward to crossing this off of my to-try list, but we will not be back.

text:
Indy's distilleries are getting better with time, and West Fork Whiskey is a testament to that. The scene has a long way to go to reach the caliber of our neighbors, but West Fork has bourbon I enjoy drinking, cocktails that are fun and innovative, a welcoming space, a knowledgeable and passionate staff, and a friendly listening ear for newcomers and regulars alike.

I was fortunate enough to start out with a flight. While you've got to be careful anytime you have so many ounces of liquor not to fall in love with the booze for the sake of booze, the variety and texture of the drinks they've crafted are stronger than any of the the others I've visited in town. 

Add to that the brews and food from Cannon Ball next door, and you've got a solid establishment that's way too close to my work for me to have much self-control. I'll be back often.

text:
I am kind of sad to give this star rating. It wasn't that the food was poor...but the service brought down our experience significantly.

We dined at Bascettis on a weeknight, so there really shouldn't have been much of an issue. It wasn't crowded, but there were a number of tables filled in the dining room. My husband and I made our order right away, but when it was all said and done, it took almost 3 hours for us to get through our meal. We waited over 30 minutes between our appetizer and main course. Happy hour was going on when we first sat down, and we both ordered a glass of wine (which was advertised as 2 for 1). We got through our first glass before the end of happy hour, but our waiter was nowhere to be seen. We figured we had a second glass coming, so we didn't worry. But then we were informed that apparently our glass was a "double pour"...and now it was past happy hour so we couldn't order another one at that price. It irked me that he had conveniently disappeared for quite a while, so there was no way we would have been able to even order a second glass.

I also have to note that a few other diners walked in the door as we were eating...one group had to awkwardly stand at the front for at least 5-10 minutes before anyone greeted and sat them.

Again, the food wasn't bad whatsoever! It was actually pretty good...but unfortunately I don't think we will be back to this restaurant since it doesn't seem like the service is up to par.

text:
I recognize Zoes Kitchen has fresh wholesome ingredients, but I've never left craving their next meal. With Italian cooking great ingredients make everything taste incredible, but this just isn't the case for Zoes Kitchen.

This location has a door near the road and a door near the middle of the parking lot. The door near the busy road is the main entrance. The door in the middle of the parking lot puts you in a very awkward, end of the line next to the cashier area when you enter using this door.

Trying to find something I like on this menu is probably my toughest battle. I see a lot of stuff that sounds 60% satisfying and a few things that I might like, but nothing I'm crazy about and nothing that sounds terrible. Also, I'm not someone that deals well with a choice from over 50 items. It's too many. Others may argue it's not, but look at hugely successful restaurants, they don't do this.

My main problem is probably only with the falafels, but they seem to ruin the whole dish. I got the falafel sandwich in the pita last time I was there and the falafel was so dry even the side of sauce couldn't save it. This was midday, so I know it couldn't have dried out. Perhaps I'm used to fried falafels and Zoe bakes her falafels?

I think the portion sizes are reasonable. But, the prices are very high. The prices are probably on par for how fresh the food is, but unfortunately people are used to lower prices for a compromise for quality. 

Overall, I'll go back when my wife wants to, but I haven't had a good time figuring out what I like. It's unfortunate for me that there are things on the menu that sound good that really aren't satisfying while also being on the rather expensive side.

text: Holy Dog Farts. This was an epically bad experience. So here I am on a Saturday with the family (wife, son, fat baby) wanting to get a little grub/booze and take in the first NFL wild card game (Bills v. Texans). We decided to give Chili's in Lithia a shot. Its my first time at this Chili's, but obviously we have all dined our fair share of Chili's warehouse-like trash food in the past.  Anyways, I waltz in like an old school gangsta and request to be seated somewhere within view of a television so that I can view said Wild Card game. Keep in mind it is 4:45PM and this hell hole is less than 25% full. The hostess goes "uhhhh, uhhhh maybe, uhhh (looks around like an asshat) yeah... uhhh, come with me". She proceeds to sit us adjacent to the bathroom in the only spot where the tv is literally in the opposite direction of my head. I pointed out a plethora of open tables  (I'm talking 12 plus) in view of several TVs. She says "No, you can't sit there because I don't have servers for those spots". I looked at the 16 year old kid and asked when she expected to have serves so I could sit there. She indicated really soon. I said coolio lamb chop, we will gladly wait here at the table with the TV view. She looked at me with an Old Testament vengeance and said "No, I will need you to wait up front".  So, with all the calmness in the world I looked at my girl and said "f*ck this". Just at that moment I noticed a booth in the bar area and went and sat down. From this point everything took forever, which was perplexing considering the place was empty and crawling with slack jawed employees. Regardless, food came wrong twice. Check out the Southwestern Egg Rolls the waitress brought us after brining us a wrong item. The egg rolls looked like a battered wife. And yes, that is the wrong dipping sauce. The waitress was so depressed I couldn't even bring my self to complain as I thought it may push her over the edge. I couldn't have that my on conscious tonight. Any other day I wouldn't have cared, but not that night. In closing, I just want to say this place blows horse wang. Thanks Chili's for giving a 0.0 effort. Drinks are incredibly cheap and therefore I will likely be back.

text:
This is the most conveniently located Rouses for me to pick up dinner ingredients after work. The parking lot is much more easily accessible (read: less crowded) then Whole Foods or the one on Veterans. It has also been renovated and is very clean and spacious. I still don't understand how the produce section baskets are organized though.  As mentioned earlier, there is a ridiculously huge wine and liquor selection. 

Yesterday, I wanted to buy a whole chicken and have it cut up for me but they didn't have any in the glass meat display (which is about half the size of the seafood display; go figure). The employee behind the counter asked if he could help me, told me to pick out a fryer and he would cut it and repackage it for me. So nice! I also noticed that they have many more pre-prepared foods (you know, like at WF, where veggies are pre-cut or meats are stuffed/marinated), but unlike the WF ones, they are much more regional in nature, such as boneless boudin-stuffed chickens. I bought some sausage-stuffed mushrooms that I can't wait to eat. 

The lines are never long indoors either. The prices aren't bad; it's not Walmart or Target cheap, of course, but they do make an effort to offer local and organic produce that is still very affordable.

text:
After a couple hours of beach v-ball, we were ready for dinner. I wanted to try a new Korean spot so decided to give Country Coco a shot. They took over from Luscious Kitchen and before that, Sambol Sri Lankan (RIP). This plaza has really become Korean with Miga, Pocha Seoul, Gagnon Street Foods and Let Eat Snow all in the same plaza.

We dropped in on a Sunday evening (around 5:00pm). It was dead inside besides several takeout orders. Service was excellent. Love how they have a button that you press to get their attention.

The menu consists of barbecue chicken, fried chicken (aka. KFC), shares & sides and a few combo options. Chicken orders takes 15-20 minutes as they're made to order. 

- Banchan - you only get side dish, cubed radish, but these were crunchy and light.
- COCO rabokki (#19) ($14.00) - our waitress warned us that this was very spicy but it was mild at best. Standard fare and you don't get much noodles. Skip it.
- French fries (#24) ($6.00) - we opted for the swing cheese ($1.50 extra). Fries were light but still had a crispy bite. The swing cheese was tasty. Would get again.
- Joomul bob (#23) ($6.00) - never had or seen this dish before. You mix up the ingredients yourself and form them into rice balls. Super tasty!
- Goon mandu (#24) ($8.00 for 5) - deep fried dumplings. Standard.
- Original purio (#12) ($18.00 for 10) - we opted for the sweet butter seasoning. One friend found it too cheesy while another thought it was delicious (and normally doesn't like the cheese topped chicken). I found the chicken was the juiciest (of the three flavours) that we tried. 
- Fried half & half (#13) ($32.00 for 20) - 10 pieces of crispy (aka. original) and 10 pieces with the yang yum sauce. The OG was a consensus favourite from our group of 5. Hot crispy batter and good crunchy texture. The yang yum was disappointing. Not much stood out about it. 

Another decent Korean find in the south side. 3.5 stars.

After years of bad service, I feel like I must have paid my dues or something. Things are looking up slightly, and believe me when I say that it's hard for me to admit (if you read the previous review, I was pretty pissed).

The recent bad:

When I finally told DirecTV to suck it and disconnected service, this somehow nullified my contract rate (approx. $30 / month). To be clear, this wasn't the bundling discount I'm referring to, but I lost that, too. No one informed me of this. The next bill I got was nearly double what I'm used to, which caused quite a bit of distress to our bank account.

The recent good:

After a QUICK chat with a knowledgeable rep, he reimbursed us for half of the overcharge (which put us at the right amount for a monthly charge) and got us a slightly lower rate than we were paying with the DirecTV bundling discount and everything (I'm assuming this is because we've been with them for a number of years). Anyway, this little gesture toward service has really improved my opinion.

Will update as needed, but I'm satisfied for now.

text:
I have a vested interest in Swarovski crystal - my mom collects it!  So every so often, a nice crystal to add to her collection makes the perfect gift!  On the latest Mother's Day, that's exactly what I was thinking.

A couple of weeks before the date I started keeping my eyes open for a nice piece.  So every time I was around a jewellery store that carries the line, I would stop in and take a look.  After the very first store, I found one that I really thought Mom would love.  So I linger around the display case for a few minutes, looking around inquisitively as if I needed help, and got ignored on every front!  Mind you there were people around, but the associates seemed to continuously skip over me and help the next person(s) who had walked into the store.  

Thinking this would be a one time thing, we walk out thinking we would go somewhere where they wanted our business.  Well interestingly enough, this exact same thing happened and each and EVERY jewellery store I walked into.  Bonnydoon, Southgate, Sherwood Park, and West Ed: every single one of them I walked into willing to purchase, and walked out (wallet untouched) because not one person offered to help me!  

HEY JEWELRY STORES: I know it's no diamond ring, but it's still an easy $200?!?!  What gives?

Although this West Ed Swarovski outlet is no match for the one I visited in Innsbruck, Austria; it certainly has the a much better selection and the prices are 10% lower than what you would pay at the jewellery store.  And by golly they actually came over and asked if they could help me with anything - obviously a foreign concept to many, but really such an easy sell!  

If you are a collector (or know a collector); save yourself some time, aggravation, a few bucks, and check this place out!

text:
I wasn't sure how I'd feel about this place since it seemed very Americanized and the bf and I had great memories of eating authentic food in Vietnam fairly recently. But I had heard so many mixed reviews that I wanted to see for myself.

Firstly, their menu is huge and overwhelming. They should really consider cutting it down a bit to make things easier, and so that they can focus on making a few dishes really really well. Just my opinion.

The banh mi is all right. My friend ordered the regular pork, not the bbq pork and I was expecting more. I also prefer my banh mis with an egg but that's a whole nother thing. 

I ordered Viet chicken salad because I had a delicious one in Vietnam but I rarely see it on menus around here. It was so good! I wish there had been a bit of a peanut crunch but the flavors were spot-on. It was all very fresh and tasty.

My friend ordered the chicken pho. It was tasty but very oily. The bf ordered a vermicelli dish with grilled pork that was well-made and he really enjoyed it. My other friend ordered a broken rice dish and her pork was rather tough and overcooked, but the flavors were also very nicely done.

Minus a star for the price, though. You can get great banh mis for $2.50-3 with a lot more stuff packed into it...is it an ambiance thing? I don't get it. The bowl of pho was also ridiculously small. Most places have a small/med/large option and rightly so. 
I thought my salad was a good price for the amount of fresh veggies, but in general, all the portions were fairly small in comparison to price. 

So yes, flavors-good, prices and portions-meh. I'd be back but only for the chicken salad. I still prefer Truc Lam's pho and I have yet to find the perfect banh mi in StL.

* FAIR WARNING: if you come here during lunch hours you will be waiting for a minute as this is the only Chik-fila in the center city/Rittenhouse area * 

I always order on my phone and walk over to pick up my order. Chik-fila is a classic and it really never disappoints. During their peak rush times you must 100% check your order (to-go or not) as they 8/10 will forget something in the bag. I'm not saying something big like a sandwich or fries I'm talking about napkins, a sauce you requested or utensils. So not a biggie per se. 

I'm a Chik-fila app member and they are always sending me free rewards so I really can't complain lol

text:
Ranked consistently as one of the top, if not THE top, airport in the US, Indianapolis International Airport is a pleasant and easily navigated airport.  This airport is clean and modern.  The layout is logical with clearly marked signs and directions for gates, terminals, ground transportation, etc.  Most importantly, this airport does not feel crowded or cramped.  It is very spacious compared to other airports which makes for a much more enjoyable experience.   The only big lines I have encountered at this airport are security (during busiest times) and Starbucks.  Moving sidewalks throughout make it easy to get from point A to point B relatively quickly.  I highly recommend stopping in at Just Pop In to grab some great local popcorn.  I like to pick up pre-packaged bags as gifts on my way to the gate.  

TSA pre-check is only offered through the terminal A side, however, you can access all gates (including terminal B) from either terminal.

text:
A friend has continued to ask me if I'd tried King of Pizza yet and kept insisting they have the best pizza in this area. They have tons of specialty slices available which is a huge plus over other pizzerias in the area when grabbing something for lunch. I tried the veggie (diced broccoli, tomatoes, onions and garlic) and a buffalo chicken slice. Both were extremely good although for me the buffalo was a little too spicy. 

Prices are very reasonable, about $3 for a specialty slice. Hubs had the white pizza with garlic and it was delicious. 

Haven't tried their wings yet which are also supposed to be world famous good. Maybe next time but it takes a lot for a pizzaholic to stray...

text:
Looking for a chill spot to grab a drink and watch the last half of game 3 of the NBA finals, we ventured in to The Funky Monk. Loved the location when it was World of Beer so why not, right?

Just a small crowd so we were able to grab a prime spot outdoors and were immediately waited on by our server. She was just friendly enough without being disinterested which works for me. The Mexi Mule was exactly what I needed - served ice cold in a copper Mule mug and very refreshing. I just wish the habanero syrup was a little more prevalent because I really couldn't taste any spice to the drink whatsoever. My guy ordered up the American Pie which is a combo of apple pie moonshine, Fireball and hard cider. I think the bartender went a little heavy on the Fireball though and it became overpowering. His drink was garnished with a couple of 100 proof cherries which he didn't care for but I loved - even got a couple extra from our server because I dropped one of his.

The food menu is really sparse which was disappointing but we managed to find something to munch on: the Nosh Board. I'm a lover of charcuterie and admittedly the Nosh Board is NOT advertised as charcuterie but it was disappointing and dull. Pretty sure there were just 2 cheeses (a cheddar that was a bit mild and what seemed like a soft mozzarella), an average salami, shaved ham, olives, grapes, pepperoncinis and couple of slices of bread drizzled in balsamic. The cheeses were dull, the meats tasted like they were from Fry's deli and the grapes were squishy. Wish they'd put more effort in to the dish and make it more interesting and flavorful.

All in all, it was a great place to hang out for an hour to watch the rest of the game and relax. I'm sure we'll be back.

text:
We've missed this place ever since moving away. So when we visited last month we had to stop by! Their Carne Asada Fries were always a go-to for us. And they still delivered for us over a year later. Their fries are nicely seasoned and are covered with chunks are juicy meat. 

I have to say that the brisket taco was far spicier than I remember, so that was jarring. But the duck taco with bbq sauce was nice. 

I'd always meant to try the corn fritters and we finally made it happen. They almost looked like hush puppies. They were nice, but not quite as flavorful as I was hoping, so I don't think I'd get them again. 

This location is very open and spacious, especially compared to The Loop and Soulard. I absolutely love the vibe from the awesome murals. Very Cool.

I will also mention that our service was not only good, but ridiculously fast! Sweet!!

Delightful place. Offers outside seating on the intercoastal and inside with great background music. Parking is premium because the place is busy but the offer valet service.

They offer varying specials and the drinks are very good. I  ordered the jumbo shrimp cocktail appetizer, very tasty,  with the shrimp served in individual shot glasses. For the main entree I had the Steakhouse Stir Fry medium to perfection seasoned with salt garnished mixed vegetables on bed or rice.  A very nice combination.  It came with a salad and bread. The salad was fresh and bread was warm accompanied with herbs and oil. Excellent all around.

Definitely a place to try for the local and the tourist alike.
